# Agent Instructions for vLLM

> These instructions apply to **all** AI-assisted contributions to `vllm-project/vllm`.
> Breaching these guidelines can result in automatic banning.

## 1. Contribution Policy (Mandatory)

### Duplicate-work checks

Before proposing a PR, run these checks:

```bash
gh issue view <issue_number> --repo vllm-project/vllm --comments
gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "<issue_number> in:body"
gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "<short area keywords>"
```

- If an open PR already addresses the same fix, do not open another.
- If your approach is materially different, explain the difference in the issue.

### No low-value busywork PRs

Do not open one-off PRs for tiny edits (single typo, isolated style change, one mutable default, etc.). Mechanical cleanups are acceptable only when bundled with substantive work.

### Accountability

- Pure code-agent PRs are **not allowed**. A human submitter must understand and defend the change end-to-end.
- The submitting human must review every changed line and run relevant tests.
- PR descriptions for AI-assisted work **must** include:
    - Why this is not duplicating an existing PR.
    - Test commands run and results.
    - Clear statement that AI assistance was used.

### Fail-closed behavior

If work is duplicate/trivial busywork, **do not proceed**. Return a short explanation of what is missing.

---

## 2. Development Workflow

- **Never use system `python3` or bare `pip`/`pip install`.** All Python commands must go through `uv` and `.venv/bin/python`.

### Environment setup

```bash
# Install `uv` if you don't have it already:
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Always use `uv` for Python environment management:
uv venv --python 3.12
source .venv/bin/activate

# Always make sure `pre-commit` and its hooks are installed:
uv pip install -r requirements/lint.txt
pre-commit install
```

### Installing dependencies

```bash
# If you are only making Python changes:
VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1 uv pip install -e . --torch-backend=auto

# If you are also making C/C++ changes:
uv pip install -e . --torch-backend=auto
```

### Running tests

> Requires [Environment setup](#environment-setup) and [Installing dependencies](#installing-dependencies).

```bash
# Install test dependencies.
# requirements/test/cuda.txt is pinned to x86_64; on other platforms, use the
# unpinned source file instead:
uv pip install -r requirements/test/cuda.in    # resolves for current platform
# Or on x86_64:
uv pip install -r requirements/test/cuda.txt

# Run a specific test file (use .venv/bin/python directly;
# `source activate` does not persist in non-interactive shells):
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/path/to/test_file.py -v
```

### Running linters

> Requires [Environment setup](#environment-setup).

```bash
# Run all pre-commit hooks on staged files:
pre-commit run

# Run on all files:
pre-commit run --all-files

# Run a specific hook:
pre-commit run ruff-check --all-files

# Run mypy as it is in CI:
pre-commit run mypy-3.10 --all-files --hook-stage manual
```

### Commit messages

Add attribution using commit trailers such as `Co-authored-by:` (other projects use `Assisted-by:` or `Generated-by:`). For example:

```text
Your commit message here

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Claude
Co-authored-by: gemini-code-assist
Signed-off-by: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
```

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2: # SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3: """TurboQuant configuration."""
4: 
5: import math
6: from dataclasses import dataclass
7: 
8: # Named TQ presets: each maps to frozen config parameters.
9: # key_quant_bits: 8 = FP8 keys, 3-4 = MSE (Lloyd-Max) quantized keys.
10: # value_quant_bits: 3-4 = uniform quantized values.
11: TQ_PRESETS: dict[str, dict] = {
12:     "turboquant_k8v4": {
13:         "key_quant_bits": 8,
14:         "value_quant_bits": 4,
15:         "norm_correction": False,
16:     },
17:     "turboquant_4bit_nc": {
18:         "key_quant_bits": 4,
19:         "value_quant_bits": 4,
20:         "norm_correction": True,
21:     },
22:     "turboquant_k3v4_nc": {
23:         "key_quant_bits": 3,
24:         "value_quant_bits": 4,
25:         "norm_correction": True,
26:     },
27:     "turboquant_3bit_nc": {
28:         "key_quant_bits": 3,
29:         "value_quant_bits": 3,
30:         "norm_correction": True,
31:     },
32: }
33: 
34: 
35: @dataclass
36: class TurboQuantConfig:
37:     """Configuration for TurboQuant KV-cache quantization.
38: 
39:     Applies Hadamard rotation followed by per-coordinate Lloyd-Max scalar
40:     quantization for keys, and uniform quantization for values.
41: 
42:     Historical note: this is the scalar case of the HIGGS quantization
43:     method (Malinovskii et al., "Pushing the Limits of Large Language Model
44:     Quantization via the Linearity Theorem", NAACL 2025; preprint
45:     arXiv:2411.17525): rotation + optimized grid + optional re-normalization,
46:     applied to KV cache compression. A first application of this approach to
47:     KV-cache compression is in "Cache Me If You Must: Adaptive Key-Value
48:     Quantization for Large Language Models" (Shutova et al., ICML 2025;
49:     preprint arXiv:2501.19392). Both these references pre-date the
50:     TurboQuant paper.
51: 
52:     QJL is intentionally omitted — community consensus (5+ independent
53:     groups) found it hurts attention quality by amplifying variance through
54:     softmax.
55: 
56:     Named presets (use via --kv-cache-dtype):
57:         turboquant_k8v4:   FP8 keys + 4-bit values, 2.6x, +1.17% PPL
58:         turboquant_4bit_nc: 4-bit MSE keys + 4-bit values + NC, 3.8x, +2.71%
59:         turboquant_k3v4_nc: 3-bit MSE keys + 4-bit values + NC, ~3.5x, +10.63%
60:         turboquant_3bit_nc: 3-bit MSE keys + 3-bit values + NC, 4.9x, +20.59%
61: 
62:     Args:
63:         head_dim: Attention head dimension (e.g. 64, 96, 128).
64:         key_quant_bits: Bits for key quantization. 8 = FP8 keys (no
65:             rotation/MSE). 3-4 = Lloyd-Max MSE quantized keys.
66:         value_quant_bits: Bits per value dimension for uniform quantization.
67:             3 = 8 levels, 4 = 16 levels (default).
68:         norm_correction: Re-normalize centroid vectors to unit norm before
69:             inverse rotation during dequant. Fixes quantization-induced norm
70:             distortion, improving PPL by ~0.8% at 4-bit.
71:     """
72: 
73:     head_dim: int = 128
74:     key_quant_bits: int = 3  # 3-4 = MSE keys, 8 = FP8 keys
75:     value_quant_bits: int = 4  # 3-4 = uniform quantized values
76:     seed: int = 42  # kept for backward compatibility; no longer used internally
77:     norm_correction: bool = False
78: 
79:     @property
80:     def key_fp8(self) -> bool:
81:         """Whether keys are stored as FP8 — no rotation/quantization needed."""
82:         return self.key_quant_bits == 8
83: 
84:     @property
85:     def mse_bits(self) -> int:
86:         """MSE quantizer bit-width (determines centroid count: 2^mse_bits).
87: 
88:         For MSE key modes, equals key_quant_bits.
89:         For FP8 key mode, falls back to value_quant_bits (centroids are still
90:         needed for continuation-prefill dequant and decode kernel params).
91:         """
92:         if self.key_fp8:
93:             return self.value_quant_bits
94:         return self.key_quant_bits
95: 
96:     @property
97:     def key_mse_bits(self) -> int:
98:         """MSE bits actually used for key quantization (0 if FP8 keys)."""
99:         if self.key_fp8:
100:             return 0
101:         return self.key_quant_bits
102: 
103:     @property
104:     def centroid_bits(self) -> int:
105:         """Bits for centroid generation — always non-zero."""
106:         return self.mse_bits
107: 
108:     @property
109:     def n_centroids(self) -> int:
110:         return 2**self.mse_bits
111: 
112:     @property
113:     def key_packed_size(self) -> int:
114:         """Packed bytes for a single KEY vector.
115: 
116:         FP8 mode (key_quant_bits=8):
117:           head_dim bytes (1 byte per element, no overhead).
118: 
119:         TQ mode:
120:           - MSE indices: ceil(head_dim * key_mse_bits / 8) bytes
121:           - vec_norm:     2 bytes (float16)
122:         """
123:         if self.key_fp8:
124:             return self.head_dim  # 1 byte per element
125:         mse_bytes = math.ceil(self.head_dim * self.key_mse_bits / 8)
126:         norm_bytes = 2  # vec_norm fp16
127:         return mse_bytes + norm_bytes
128: 
129:     @property
130:     def effective_value_quant_bits(self) -> int:
131:         """Actual bits used for value storage."""
132:         return self.value_quant_bits
133: 
134:     @property
135:     def value_packed_size(self) -> int:
136:         """Packed bytes for a single VALUE vector.
137: 
138:         Uniform quantization: ceil(head_dim * bits / 8) + 4 bytes (scale + zero fp16).
139:         """
140:         data_bytes = math.ceil(self.head_dim * self.value_quant_bits / 8)
141:         return data_bytes + 4  # +2 scale(fp16) +2 zero(fp16)
142: 
143:     @property
144:     def slot_size(self) -> int:
145:         """Total packed bytes per head per position (key + value combined).
146: 
147:         Layout: [key_packed | value_packed]
148:         """
149:         return self.key_packed_size + self.value_packed_size
150: 
151:     @property
152:     def slot_size_aligned(self) -> int:
153:         """Slot size rounded up to next even number.
154: 
155:         Even-number is required so effective_head_size = slot_size_aligned // 2
156:         is integral.
157:         """
158:         s = self.slot_size
159:         return s + (s % 2)  # round up to even
160: 
161:     @staticmethod
162:     def get_boundary_skip_layers(num_layers: int, n: int = 2) -> list[str]:
163:         """Get layer indices to skip TQ compression (boundary protection).
164: 
165:         Returns first N and last N layer indices as strings, suitable for
166:         kv_cache_dtype_skip_layers.
167:         """
168:         if n <= 0 or num_layers <= 0:
169:             return []
170:         n = min(n, num_layers // 2)  # don't skip more than half
171:         first = list(range(n))
172:         last = list(range(num_layers - n, num_layers))
173:         # Deduplicate (if num_layers <= 2*n)
174:         indices = sorted(set(first + last))
175:         return [str(i) for i in indices]
176: 
177:     @staticmethod
178:     def from_cache_dtype(cache_dtype: str, head_dim: int) -> "TurboQuantConfig":
179:         """Create config from a named preset.
180: 
181:         Valid presets: turboquant_k8v4, turboquant_4bit_nc, etc.
182:         """
183:         if cache_dtype not in TQ_PRESETS:
184:             valid = ", ".join(TQ_PRESETS.keys())
185:             raise ValueError(
186:                 f"Unknown TurboQuant cache dtype: {cache_dtype!r}. "
187:                 f"Valid presets: {valid}"
188:             )
189:         preset = TQ_PRESETS[cache_dtype]
190:         return TurboQuantConfig(
191:             head_dim=head_dim,
192:             key_quant_bits=preset["key_quant_bits"],
193:             value_quant_bits=preset["value_quant_bits"],
194:             norm_correction=preset["norm_correction"],
195:         )

(End of file - total 195 lines)
</content>

<path>D:\Desktop\Tuborquant\vllm-0.20.2\vllm\v1\attention\ops\triton_turboquant_decode.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
340:     NUM_KV_HEADS: tl.constexpr,
341:     MSE_BYTES: tl.constexpr,
342:     KPS: tl.constexpr,
343:     VQB: tl.constexpr,
344:     VAL_DATA_BYTES: tl.constexpr,
345:     MSE_BITS: tl.constexpr,
346:     KEY_FP8: tl.constexpr,
347:     BLOCK_D: tl.constexpr,
348:     NORM_CORRECTION: tl.constexpr = 0,
349:     FP8_E4B15: tl.constexpr = 0,  # 1 = use e4b15 (Ampere/Ada), 0 = e4nv (Hopper+)
350: ):
351:     """Full dequant: reconstruct K (MSE centroids * norm or FP8) and V to fp16."""
352:     pos = tl.program_id(0)
353:     bh = tl.program_id(1)
354:     bid = bh // NUM_KV_HEADS
355:     hid = bh % NUM_KV_HEADS
356: 
357:     page_idx = pos // BLOCK_SIZE
358:     page_off = pos % BLOCK_SIZE
359:     block_num = tl.load(Block_table_ptr + bid * stride_bt_b + page_idx).to(tl.int64)
360:     slot_base = (
361:         block_num * stride_cache_block
362:         + tl.cast(page_off, tl.int64) * stride_cache_pos
363:         + tl.cast(hid, tl.int64) * stride_cache_head
364:     )
365: 
366:     d_offs = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_D)
367:     d_mask = d_offs < HEAD_DIM
368: 
369:     # === K dequant ===
370:     ko_base = bid * stride_ko_b + hid * stride_ko_h + pos * stride_ko_s
371:     if KEY_FP8:
372:         k_raw = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + d_offs, mask=d_mask, other=0)
373:         if FP8_E4B15:
374:             k_recon = k_raw.to(tl.float8e4b15, bitcast=True).to(tl.float32)
375:         else:
376:             k_recon = k_raw.to(tl.float8e4nv, bitcast=True).to(tl.float32)
377:         tl.store(K_out_ptr + ko_base + d_offs, k_recon.to(tl.float16), mask=d_mask)
378:     else:
379:         # MSE unpack (3-bit or 4-bit) + norms
380:         mse_bit_off = d_offs * MSE_BITS
381:         mse_byte_idx = mse_bit_off // 8
382:         mse_bit_shift = mse_bit_off % 8
383:         mse_umask = (1 << MSE_BITS) - 1
384: 
385:         mse_raw0 = tl.load(
386:             KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + mse_byte_idx, mask=d_mask, other=0
387:         ).to(tl.int32)
388:         mse_raw1 = tl.load(
389:             KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + mse_byte_idx + 1, mask=d_mask, other=0
390:         ).to(tl.int32)
391:         raw16_key = mse_raw0 | (mse_raw1 << 8)
392:         mse_idx = (raw16_key >> mse_bit_shift) & mse_umask
393: 
394:         k_mse = tl.load(Centroids_ptr + mse_idx, mask=d_mask, other=0.0)
395: 
396:         # Norm correction: re-normalize centroid vector to unit norm
397:         if NORM_CORRECTION:
398:             c_norm_sq = tl.sum(tl.where(d_mask, k_mse * k_mse, 0.0), axis=0)
399:             c_inv_norm = 1.0 / tl.sqrt(c_norm_sq + 1e-16)
400:             k_mse = k_mse * c_inv_norm
401: 
402:         # Norms at MSE_BYTES offset (no QJL bytes)
403:         norm_base = slot_base + MSE_BYTES
404:         n_lo = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + norm_base).to(tl.uint16)
405:         n_hi = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + norm_base + 1).to(tl.uint16)
406:         vec_norm = (n_lo | (n_hi << 8)).to(tl.float16, bitcast=True).to(tl.float32)
407: 
408:         k_recon = vec_norm * k_mse
409:         tl.store(K_out_ptr + ko_base + d_offs, k_recon.to(tl.float16), mask=d_mask)
410: 
411:     # === V dequant ===
412:     val_base = slot_base + KPS
413:     if VQB == 4:
414:         vb_idx = d_offs // 2
415:         vb_shift = (d_offs % 2) * 4
416:         val_raw = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + val_base + vb_idx, mask=d_mask, other=0).to(
417:             tl.int32
418:         )
419:         v_idx = ((val_raw >> vb_shift) & 0xF).to(tl.float32)
420: 
421:         sc_base = val_base + VAL_DATA_BYTES
422:         sc_lo = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base).to(tl.uint16)
423:         sc_hi = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base + 1).to(tl.uint16)
424:         v_scale = (sc_lo | (sc_hi << 8)).to(tl.float16, bitcast=True).to(tl.float32)
425:         zr_lo = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base + 2).to(tl.uint16)
426:         zr_hi = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base + 3).to(tl.uint16)
427:         v_zero = (zr_lo | (zr_hi << 8)).to(tl.float16, bitcast=True).to(tl.float32)
428:         v_vals = v_idx * v_scale + v_zero
429:     elif VQB == 3:
430:         # 3-bit value unpack: 8 values per 3 bytes
431:         val_bit_off = d_offs * 3
432:         val_byte_idx = val_bit_off // 8
433:         val_bit_shift = val_bit_off % 8
434:         val_raw0 = tl.load(
435:             KV_cache_ptr + val_base + val_byte_idx, mask=d_mask, other=0
436:         ).to(tl.int32)
437:         val_raw1 = tl.load(
438:             KV_cache_ptr + val_base + val_byte_idx + 1, mask=d_mask, other=0
439:         ).to(tl.int32)
440:         raw16_val = val_raw0 | (val_raw1 << 8)
441:         v_idx = ((raw16_val >> val_bit_shift) & 0x7).to(tl.float32)
442: 
443:         sc_base = val_base + VAL_DATA_BYTES
444:         sc_lo = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base).to(tl.uint16)
445:         sc_hi = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base + 1).to(tl.uint16)
446:         v_scale = (sc_lo | (sc_hi << 8)).to(tl.float16, bitcast=True).to(tl.float32)
447:         zr_lo = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base + 2).to(tl.uint16)
448:         zr_hi = tl.load(KV_cache_ptr + sc_base + 3).to(tl.uint16)
449:         v_zero = (zr_lo | (zr_hi << 8)).to(tl.float16, bitcast=True).to(tl.float32)
450:         v_vals = v_idx * v_scale + v_zero
451:     else:
452:         v_vals = tl.zeros([BLOCK_D], dtype=tl.float32)
453: 
454:     vo_base = bid * stride_vo_b + hid * stride_vo_h + pos * stride_vo_s
455:     tl.store(V_out_ptr + vo_base + d_offs, v_vals.to(tl.float16), mask=d_mask)
456:

<content>
100:         )
101:         val_min = tl.min(tl.where(d_mask, val_vec, float("inf")), axis=0)
102:         val_max = tl.max(tl.where(d_mask, val_vec, -float("inf")), axis=0)
103:         v_scale = (val_max - val_min) / 15.0
104:         v_scale = tl.where(v_scale > 1e-8, v_scale, 1e-8)
105: 
106:         # Quantize all D elements from register (no re-load)
107:         q_all = tl.minimum(
108:             tl.maximum(((val_vec - val_min) / v_scale + 0.5).to(tl.int32), 0), 15
109:         )
110:         # Reshape to pairs and pack two 4-bit values per byte
111:         q_pairs = tl.reshape(q_all, [BLOCK_D // 2, 2])
112:         shifts_4 = tl.arange(0, 2) * 4
113:         packed_val = tl.sum((q_pairs & 0xF) << shifts_4[None, :], axis=1).to(tl.uint8)
114:         val_offs = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_D // 2)
115:         val_mask = val_offs < VAL_DATA_BYTES
116:         tl.store(
117:             KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + val_cache_offset + val_offs,
118:             packed_val,
119:             mask=val_mask,
120:         )
121: 
122:         sc_offset = val_cache_offset + VAL_DATA_BYTES
123:         sc_f16 = v_scale.to(tl.float16)
124:         sc_u16 = sc_f16.to(tl.uint16, bitcast=True)
125:         tl.store(KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + sc_offset, (sc_u16 & 0xFF).to(tl.uint8))
126:         tl.store(
127:             KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + sc_offset + 1,
128:             ((sc_u16 >> 8) & 0xFF).to(tl.uint8),
129:         )
130:         zr_f16 = val_min.to(tl.float16)
131:         zr_u16 = zr_f16.to(tl.uint16, bitcast=True)
132:         tl.store(KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + sc_offset + 2, (zr_u16 & 0xFF).to(tl.uint8))
133:         tl.store(
134:             KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + sc_offset + 3,
135:             ((zr_u16 >> 8) & 0xFF).to(tl.uint8),
136:         )
137: 
138: 
139: # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
140: # FP8 key store + value uniform quantization
141: # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
142: 
143: 
144: @triton.jit
145: def _tq_fused_store_fp8(
146:     Key_ptr,  # [NH, D] float16/bfloat16 — raw keys
147:     Value_ptr,  # [NH, D] float16/bfloat16 — raw values
148:     KV_cache_ptr,  # [total_bytes] uint8 (flattened view)
149:     Slot_mapping_ptr,  # [N] int32 — per-token slot indices
150:     # Cache strides (for computing byte offsets)
151:     stride_cache_block: tl.constexpr,
152:     stride_cache_pos: tl.constexpr,
153:     stride_cache_head: tl.constexpr,
154:     # Dimensions
155:     D: tl.constexpr,
156:     H: tl.constexpr,
157:     BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,
158:     BLOCK_D: tl.constexpr,
159:     # TQ layout
160:     KPS: tl.constexpr,
161:     # Value quantization
162:     VQB: tl.constexpr,
163:     VAL_DATA_BYTES: tl.constexpr,
164:     # Packing block sizes
165:     BLOCK_VAL: tl.constexpr,
166:     BLOCK_GRP: tl.constexpr = 16,
167:     FP8_E4B15: tl.constexpr = 0,  # 1 = e4b15 (Ampere/Ada), 0 = e4nv (Hopper+)
168: ):
169:     """FP8 key cast+scatter + value uniform quantization."""
170:     pid = tl.program_id(0)
171:     token_idx = pid // H
172:     head_idx = pid % H
173: 
174:     slot = tl.load(Slot_mapping_ptr + token_idx)
175:     if slot < 0:
176:         return
177:     blk = (slot // BLOCK_SIZE).to(tl.int64)
178:     off = (slot % BLOCK_SIZE).to(tl.int64)
179:     head_idx_i64 = tl.cast(head_idx, tl.int64)
180:     slot_base = (
181:         blk * stride_cache_block
182:         + off * stride_cache_pos
183:         + head_idx_i64 * stride_cache_head
184:     )
185: 
186:     base = pid * D
187: 
188:     # ── FP8 KEY: cast to FP8 in-kernel and store ─────────────────
189:     d_offs = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_D)
190:     d_mask = d_offs < D
191:     k_vals = tl.load(Key_ptr + base + d_offs, mask=d_mask, other=0.0)
192:     k_fp8 = k_vals.to(tl.float8e4b15) if FP8_E4B15 else k_vals.to(tl.float8e4nv)
193:     k_bytes = k_fp8.to(tl.uint8, bitcast=True)
194:     tl.store(KV_cache_ptr + slot_base + d_offs, k_bytes, mask=d_mask)
195: 
196:     # ── VALUE QUANTIZE + PACK ───────────────────────────────────────
197:     _store_quantized_value(
198:         Value_ptr,
199:         KV_cache_ptr,
200:         base,
201:         slot_base,
202:         d_offs,
203:         d_mask,
204:         D=D,
205:         KPS=KPS,
206:         VQB=VQB,
207:         VAL_DATA_BYTES=VAL_DATA_BYTES,
208:         BLOCK_D=BLOCK_D,
209:         BLOCK_VAL=BLOCK_VAL,
210:         BLOCK_GRP=BLOCK_GRP,
211:     )
212: 
213: 
214: # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
215: # Fused MSE store: bucketize + MSE index pack + norm store + value pack
216: # (eliminates 4 PyTorch kernel launches per layer vs pack-only kernel)
217: # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
218: 
219: 
220: @triton.jit
221: def _tq_fused_store_mse(
222:     # Post-rotation inputs
223:     Y_ptr,  # [NH, D] float32 — rotated normalized keys (x_hat @ PiT)
224:     Norms_ptr,  # [NH] float32 — key vector norms (||k||)
225:     Value_ptr,  # [NH, D] float32 — raw values
226:     # Quantization tables
227:     Midpoints_ptr,  # [n_centroids-1] float32
228:     # Cache and indexing
229:     KV_cache_ptr,  # [total_bytes] uint8 (flattened view)
230:     Slot_mapping_ptr,  # [N] int32 — per-token slot indices
231:     # Cache strides
232:     stride_cache_block: tl.constexpr,
233:     stride_cache_pos: tl.constexpr,
234:     stride_cache_head: tl.constexpr,
235:     # Dimensions
236:     D: tl.constexpr,
237:     H: tl.constexpr,
238:     BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,
239:     BLOCK_D: tl.constexpr,
240:     # TQ layout
241:     MSE_BYTES: tl.constexpr,
242:     KPS: tl.constexpr,
243:     # Value quantization
244:     VQB: tl.constexpr,
245:     VAL_DATA_BYTES: tl.constexpr,
246:     # Packing block sizes
247:     BLOCK_VAL: tl.constexpr,
248:     # MSE params
249:     MSE_BITS: tl.constexpr,
250:     N_CENTROIDS: tl.constexpr,
251:     BLOCK_GRP: tl.constexpr = 16,
252: ):
253:     """Fused MSE quantize + pack + store.
254: 
255:     Performs binary-search bucketize, MSE index packing, norm storage,
256:     and value quantization in one kernel.
257:     """
258:     pid = tl.program_id(0)
259:     token_idx = pid // H
260:     head_idx = pid % H
261: 
262:     slot = tl.load(Slot_mapping_ptr + token_idx)
263:     if slot < 0:
264:         return
265:     blk = (slot // BLOCK_SIZE).to(tl.int64)
266:     off = (slot % BLOCK_SIZE).to(tl.int64)
267:     head_idx_i64 = tl.cast(head_idx, tl.int64)
268:     slot_base = (
269:         blk * stride_cache_block
270:         + off * stride_cache_pos
271:         + head_idx_i64 * stride_cache_head
272:     )
273: 
274:     base = pid * D
275:     d_offs = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_D)
276:     d_mask = d_offs < D
277: 
278:     # ── 1. BINARY SEARCH BUCKETIZE ───────────────────────────────────
279:     # Midpoints are sorted (N_CENTROIDS-1 values); binary search finds
280:     # insertion point in MSE_BITS iterations vs N_CENTROIDS-1 for linear.
281:     y_vec = tl.load(Y_ptr + base + d_offs, mask=d_mask, other=0.0)
282:     lo = tl.zeros([BLOCK_D], dtype=tl.int32)
283:     hi = tl.full([BLOCK_D], N_CENTROIDS - 1, dtype=tl.int32)
284:     for _ in range(MSE_BITS):
285:         mid = (lo + hi) >> 1
286:         # Clamp to valid midpoint index [0, N_CENTROIDS-2] for load safety;
287:         # the search result (lo) is still correct since converged lanes
288:         # don't change.
289:         safe_mid = tl.minimum(mid, N_CENTROIDS - 2)
290:         mid_val = tl.load(Midpoints_ptr + safe_mid, mask=d_mask, other=0.0)
291:         lo = tl.where(y_vec >= mid_val, mid + 1, lo)
292:         hi = tl.where(y_vec >= mid_val, hi, mid)
293:     idx = tl.minimum(lo, N_CENTROIDS - 1)
294: 
295:     # ── 2. PACK MSE INDICES from register idx ─────────────────────────
296:     if MSE_BITS == 4:
297:         idx_pairs = tl.reshape(idx, [BLOCK_D // 2, 2])
298:         shifts_4 = tl.arange(0, 2) * 4
299:         packed = tl.sum((idx_pairs & 0xF) << shifts_4[None, :], axis=1).to(tl.uint8)

(Showing lines 100-299 of 447. Use offset=300 to continue.)
</content>

57: # Continuation prefill: for small continuation chunks (q_len ≤ threshold),
58: # use the TQ decode kernel directly instead of full-dequant + flash_attn.
59: # do_kv_cache_update already stored all tokens to TQ cache, so the decode
60: # kernel can read them efficiently. This avoids O(cached_len) dequant work
61: # per continuation, eliminating the O(N²/chunk_size) collapse at long context.
62: _CONTINUATION_DECODE_THRESHOLD = 128

64: 
65: def _build_hadamard(d: int, device_str: str) -> torch.Tensor:
66:     """Orthonormal Hadamard matrix (Sylvester construction), cached per (d, device).
67: 
68:     Precomputed D×D matrix enables matmul-based WHT — single cuBLAS GEMM
69:     instead of log2(D) butterfly kernel launches. 64KB for D=128.
70:     """
71:     # Normalize device string so "cuda" and "cuda:0" hit the same cache entry.
72:     return _build_hadamard_cached(d, str(torch.device(device_str)))
73: 
74: 
75: @functools.cache
76: def _build_hadamard_cached(d: int, device_str: str) -> torch.Tensor:
77:     H = torch.tensor([[1.0]])
78:     while H.shape[0] < d:
79:         H = torch.cat([torch.cat([H, H], 1), torch.cat([H, -H], 1)], 0)
80:     return (H / math.sqrt(d)).to(torch.device(device_str))
81: 
82: 
83: class TurboQuantAttentionBackend(AttentionBackend):
84:     """Attention backend using TurboQuant KV-cache compression."""

132:         """Combined K+V cache shape — no leading 2 dimension.
133: 
134:         Standard attention backends use (2, num_blocks, block_size, num_kv_heads,
135:         head_dim) with a leading 2 to separate K and V. TurboQuant packs K+V
136:         into a single interleaved slot per head per position, so the cache is:
137: 
138:             (num_blocks, block_size, num_kv_heads, slot_size_aligned)
139: 
140:         Each slot = [key_packed | value_packed | padding].

142:         never shares cache tensors with other backends. Layers that fall back
143:         to native dtype via kv_cache_dtype_skip_layers get their own
144:         standard-shaped cache allocation.
145: 
146:         head_size is the model's real head_dim. slot_size_aligned is computed
147:         from the TQ config to ensure correct cache allocation for all head dims.
148:         """

344: 
345: # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
346: # Launcher
347: # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
348: 
349: 
350: def triton_turboquant_store(
351:     key: torch.Tensor,  # [N, H, D] — raw keys (post-RoPE)
352:     value: torch.Tensor,  # [N, H, D] — raw values
353:     kv_cache: torch.Tensor,  # [num_blocks, block_size, Hk, padded_slot] uint8
354:     slot_mapping: torch.Tensor,  # [N] int32
355:     PiT: torch.Tensor,  # [D, D] float32
356:     midpoints: torch.Tensor,  # [n_centroids-1] float32
357:     mse_bits: int,
358:     key_packed_size: int,
359:     value_quant_bits: int,
360:     key_fp8: bool = False,
361: ):
362:     """Launch TQ store kernel (FP8 or MSE path)."""
363:     N, H, D = key.shape
364:     NH = N * H
365:     block_size = kv_cache.shape[1]
366:     BLOCK_D = triton.next_power_of_2(D)
367:     mse_bytes = math.ceil(D * mse_bits / 8)
368:     n_centroids = 2**mse_bits
369: 
370:     val_data_bytes = math.ceil(D * value_quant_bits / 8)
371: 
372:     BLOCK_VAL = triton.next_power_of_2(val_data_bytes)
373: 
374:     # Cache strides (element_size=1 for uint8, so stride in bytes = stride())
375:     stride_block = kv_cache.stride(0)
376:     stride_pos = kv_cache.stride(1)
377:     stride_head = kv_cache.stride(2)
378: 
379:     block_grp = triton.next_power_of_2(D // 8) if D >= 8 else 1
380: 
381:     # ── FP8 PATH: in-kernel FP8 cast + scatter via fp8 kernel ──
382:     if key_fp8:
383:         k_flat = key.reshape(NH, D).contiguous()
384:         v_flat = value.reshape(NH, D).contiguous()
385: 
386:         fp8_e4b15 = _use_fp8_e4b15(key.device.index or 0)
387: 
388:         grid = (NH,)
389:         _tq_fused_store_fp8[grid](
390:             k_flat,
391:             v_flat,
392:             kv_cache.view(-1),
393:             slot_mapping,
394:             stride_cache_block=stride_block,
395:             stride_cache_pos=stride_pos,
396:             stride_cache_head=stride_head,
397:             D=D,
398:             H=H,
399:             BLOCK_SIZE=block_size,
400:             BLOCK_D=BLOCK_D,
401:             KPS=key_packed_size,
402:             VQB=value_quant_bits,
403:             VAL_DATA_BYTES=val_data_bytes,
404:             BLOCK_VAL=BLOCK_VAL,
405:             BLOCK_GRP=block_grp,
406:             FP8_E4B15=fp8_e4b15,
407:             num_warps=4,
408:             num_stages=1,
409:         )
410:         return
411: 
412:     # ── MSE PATH: external GEMM + fused bucketize/pack kernel ──
413:     # Normalize + rotation GEMM externally (cuBLAS is faster than in-kernel)
414:     k_flat = key.float().reshape(NH, D)
415:     norms = k_flat.norm(dim=1, keepdim=True)
416:     x_hat = k_flat / (norms + 1e-8)
417:     y = x_hat @ PiT
418: 
419:     v_flat = value.float().reshape(NH, D)
420: 
421:     # Fused kernel: bucketize + MSE index pack + norm store + value pack
422:     grid = (NH,)
423:     _tq_fused_store_mse[grid](
424:         y,
425:         norms.squeeze(1),
426:         v_flat,
427:         midpoints,
428:         kv_cache.view(-1),
429:         slot_mapping,
430:         stride_cache_block=stride_block,
431:         stride_cache_pos=stride_pos,
432:         stride_cache_head=stride_head,
433:         D=D,
434:         H=H,
435:         BLOCK_SIZE=block_size,
436:         BLOCK_D=BLOCK_D,
437:         MSE_BYTES=mse_bytes,
438:         KPS=key_packed_size,
439:         VQB=value_quant_bits,
440:         VAL_DATA_BYTES=val_data_bytes,
441:         BLOCK_VAL=BLOCK_VAL,
442:         MSE_BITS=mse_bits,
443:         N_CENTROIDS=n_centroids,
444:         BLOCK_GRP=block_grp,
445:         num_warps=4,
446:         num_stages=1,
447:     )

168: 
169: @dataclass
170: class TurboQuantMetadata(AttentionMetadata):
171:     """Metadata for TurboQuant attention."""
172: 
173:     seq_lens: torch.Tensor  # (num_reqs,) — total context length per request
174:     slot_mapping: torch.Tensor  # (num_tokens,) — cache slot for each token
175:     block_table: torch.Tensor  # (num_reqs, max_num_blocks)
176:     query_start_loc: torch.Tensor  # (num_reqs + 1,) — cu_seqlens for queries
177:     num_actual_tokens: int = 0  # actual tokens (excluding padding)
178:     max_query_len: int = 0  # longest query in batch
179:     max_seq_len: int = 0  # longest context in batch
180:     is_prefill: bool = False
181:     num_decodes: int = 0  # number of decode requests (first in batch)
182:     num_decode_tokens: int = 0  # tokens from decode requests
183: 
184: 
185: class TurboQuantMetadataBuilder(AttentionMetadataBuilder[TurboQuantMetadata]):
186:     """Builds TurboQuantMetadata from scheduler output."""
187: 
188:     _cudagraph_support: ClassVar[AttentionCGSupport] = AttentionCGSupport.UNIFORM_BATCH
189: 
190:     def __init__(self, kv_cache_spec, layer_names, vllm_config, device):
191:         super().__init__(kv_cache_spec, layer_names, vllm_config, device)
192:         self._init_reorder_batch_threshold(1, supports_spec_as_decode=False)
193: 
194:     def build_for_cudagraph_capture(
195:         self, common_attn_metadata: CommonAttentionMetadata
196:     ) -> TurboQuantMetadata:
197:         attn_metadata = self.build(0, common_attn_metadata)
198:         # Set seq_lens to 1 so CUDA graph capture is fast
199:         # (real seq_lens are filled at replay time).
200:         attn_metadata.seq_lens.fill_(1)
201:         return attn_metadata
202: 
203:     def build(self, common_prefix_len, common_attn_metadata, fast_build=False):
204:         """Build TurboQuantMetadata from common attention metadata."""
205:         cam = common_attn_metadata
206: 
207:         # With reorder_batch_threshold=1, the model runner guarantees
208:         # decodes come first in the batch. split_decodes_and_prefills
209:         # finds the boundary (operates on CPU tensors — no GPU sync).
210:         assert self.reorder_batch_threshold is not None
211:         num_decodes, num_prefills, num_decode_tokens, _ = split_decodes_and_prefills(
212:             cam, decode_threshold=self.reorder_batch_threshold
213:         )
214: 
215:         return TurboQuantMetadata(
216:             seq_lens=cam.seq_lens,
217:             slot_mapping=cam.slot_mapping,
218:             block_table=cam.block_table_tensor,
219:             query_start_loc=cam.query_start_loc,
220:             num_actual_tokens=cam.num_actual_tokens,
221:             max_query_len=cam.max_query_len,
222:             max_seq_len=cam.max_seq_len,
223:             is_prefill=(cam.max_query_len > 1),
224:             num_decodes=num_decodes,
225:             num_decode_tokens=num_decode_tokens,
226:         )
227: 
228: 
229: class TurboQuantAttentionImpl(AttentionImpl["TurboQuantMetadata"]):
230:     """TurboQuant attention implementation.
231: 
232:     Vectorized PyTorch: batch quantize/store, vectorized bit-unpack
233:     decode with einsum scores and value gather.
234:     """
235: 
236:     supports_quant_query_input: bool = False
237: 
238:     def __init__(
239:         self,
240:         num_heads: int,
241:         head_size: int,
242:         scale: float,
243:         num_kv_heads: int | None = None,
244:         alibi_slopes: list[float] | None = None,
245:         sliding_window: int | None = None,
246:         kv_cache_dtype: str = "auto",
247:         logits_soft_cap: float | None = None,
248:         attn_type: str = AttentionType.DECODER,
249:         kv_sharing_target_layer_name: str | None = None,
250:         **kwargs,
251:     ):
252:         self.num_heads = num_heads
253:         self.head_size = head_size
254:         self.scale = scale
255:         self.num_kv_heads = num_kv_heads if num_kv_heads is not None else num_heads
256:         self.num_kv_groups = num_heads // self.num_kv_heads
257:         self.kv_cache_dtype = kv_cache_dtype
258: 
259:         from vllm.model_executor.layers.quantization.turboquant.config import (
260:             TurboQuantConfig,
261:         )
262: 
263:         self.tq_config = TurboQuantConfig.from_cache_dtype(kv_cache_dtype, head_size)
264: 
265:         # Pre-compute kernel constants from config (avoid repeated arithmetic)
266:         cfg = self.tq_config
267:         self._mse_bytes = (
268:             math.ceil(head_size * cfg.key_mse_bits / 8)
269:             if not cfg.key_fp8
270:             else head_size
271:         )
272:         self._val_data_bytes = math.ceil(head_size * cfg.effective_value_quant_bits / 8)
273:         self._n_centroids = cfg.n_centroids if not cfg.key_fp8 else 1
274: 
275:         # Detect flash-attn version (FA2/3/4) for prefill paths.
276:         self.fa_version = get_flash_attn_version(head_size=head_size)
277: 
278:         # Fixed NUM_KV_SPLITS (grid dims must be constant for cudagraph,
279:         # and benchmarks show no regression vs dynamic in eager mode).
280:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
281:         self.max_num_kv_splits = (
282:             vllm_config.attention_config.tq_max_kv_splits_for_cuda_graph
283:         )
284: 
285:     def _flash_attn_varlen(
286:         self,
287:         q: torch.Tensor,
288:         k: torch.Tensor,
289:         v: torch.Tensor,
290:         cu_seqlens_q: torch.Tensor,
291:         cu_seqlens_k: torch.Tensor,
292:         max_seqlen_q: int,
293:         max_seqlen_k: int,
294:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
295:         # fa_utils.get_flash_attn_version() returns None on backends that
296:         # should not pass an explicit fa_version kwarg.
297:         if self.fa_version is None:
298:             return flash_attn_varlen_func(
299:                 q=q,
300:                 k=k,
301:                 v=v,
302:                 cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens_q,
303:                 cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens_k,
304:                 max_seqlen_q=max_seqlen_q,

306:                 softmax_scale=self.scale,
307:                 causal=True,
308:             )

311:             k=k,
312:             v=v,
313:             cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens_q,
314:             cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens_k,
315:             max_seqlen_q=max_seqlen_q,
316:             max_seqlen_k=max_seqlen_k,
317:             softmax_scale=self.scale,
318:             causal=True,
319:             fa_version=self.fa_version,
320:         )
321: 
322:     def _ensure_on_device(self, layer, device):
323:         """One-time derivation of TQ buffers (rotation matrix, midpoints).
324: 
325:         The Hadamard rotation is shared across all layers: random sign
326:         flips do not improve Lloyd-Max quantization quality because the
327:         quantizer is symmetric around zero (sign-flipping a coordinate
328:         maps it to the mirror centroid with identical distortion).
329:         """
330:         if not hasattr(layer, "_tq_cached"):
331:             D = self.head_size
332: 
333:             # Pure Hadamard: orthonormal + symmetric (H = H^T), enabling
334:             # in-kernel butterfly fusion and trivial inverse for continuation.
335:             H = _build_hadamard(D, str(device))
336:             layer._tq_PiT = H
337:             layer._tq_Pi = H
338: 
339:             c = layer._tq_centroids.to(device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
340:             c_sorted, _ = c.sort()
341:             layer._tq_midpoints = (c_sorted[:-1] + c_sorted[1:]) / 2
342:             layer._tq_cached = True
343: 
344:     def do_kv_cache_update(
345:         self,
346:         layer: torch.nn.Module,
347:         key: torch.Tensor,
348:         value: torch.Tensor,
349:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,

350:         slot_mapping: torch.Tensor,

352:         """Store compressed K/V into the combined TQ cache.
353: 
354:         Called as a separate custom op (unified_kv_cache_update) BEFORE

357:         """
358:         N = slot_mapping.shape[0]
359:         if N <= 0:
360:             return
361: 
362:         device = key.device
363:         self._ensure_on_device(layer, device)
364: 
365:         k = key[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)

368: 
369:     def forward(
370:         self,
371:         layer: AttentionLayer,
372:         query: torch.Tensor,
373:         key: torch.Tensor,
374:         value: torch.Tensor,
375:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
376:         attn_metadata: "TurboQuantMetadata",
377:         output: torch.Tensor | None = None,
378:         output_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
379:         output_block_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
380:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
381:         num_tokens = query.shape[0]
382: 
383:         if output is None:
384:             output = torch.zeros(
385:                 num_tokens,
386:                 self.num_heads * self.head_size,
387:                 dtype=query.dtype,
388:                 device=query.device,
389:             )
390: 
391:         if attn_metadata is None:
392:             return output.fill_(0)
393: 
394:         # Slice to actual tokens
395:         N = attn_metadata.num_actual_tokens
396:         if N <= 0:
397:             return output.fill_(0)
398: 
399:         q = query[:N].view(N, self.num_heads, self.head_size)
400: 
401:         # Get TQ buffers, ensure on device (one-time migration).
402:         # Use Any-typed alias for dynamic _tq_* attrs set by _ensure_on_device.
403:         tq_layer: Any = layer
404:         device = q.device
405:         self._ensure_on_device(tq_layer, device)
406:         Pi = tq_layer._tq_Pi
407:         PiT = tq_layer._tq_PiT
408:         centroids = tq_layer._tq_centroids
409: 
410:         # Compute attention (KV cache was already updated by do_kv_cache_update)
411:         # With reorder_batch_threshold=1, decodes come first in the batch.
412:         # num_decodes/num_decode_tokens from metadata give the split point.
413:         num_decodes = attn_metadata.num_decodes
414:         num_decode_tokens = attn_metadata.num_decode_tokens
415: 
416:         if not attn_metadata.is_prefill:
417:             # Pure decode batch — fast path
418:             attn_out = self._decode_attention(
419:                 q, kv_cache, attn_metadata, Pi, centroids, PiT, layer
420:             )
421:         elif num_decodes == 0:
422:             # Pure prefill batch
423:             k = key[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
424:             v = value[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
425:             attn_out = self._prefill_attention(
426:                 q,
427:                 k,
428:                 v,
429:                 kv_cache,
430:                 attn_metadata,
431:                 Pi,
432:                 centroids,
433:                 PiT,
434:                 layer=layer,
435:             )
436:         else:
437:             # Mixed batch: decodes first (guaranteed by reorder_batch).
438:             attn_out = torch.zeros(
439:                 N, self.num_heads, self.head_size, device=device, dtype=q.dtype
440:             )
441: 
442:             # --- Decode portion (first num_decodes requests) ---
443:             # Use full-batch max_seq_len as safe upper bound (no GPU sync).
444:             decode_meta = TurboQuantMetadata(
445:                 seq_lens=attn_metadata.seq_lens[:num_decodes],
446:                 slot_mapping=attn_metadata.slot_mapping[:num_decode_tokens],
447:                 block_table=attn_metadata.block_table[:num_decodes],
448:                 query_start_loc=attn_metadata.query_start_loc[: num_decodes + 1],
449:                 num_actual_tokens=num_decode_tokens,
450:                 max_query_len=1,
451:                 max_seq_len=attn_metadata.max_seq_len,
452:                 is_prefill=False,
453:             )
454:             attn_out[:num_decode_tokens] = self._decode_attention(
455:                 q[:num_decode_tokens], kv_cache, decode_meta, Pi, centroids, PiT, layer
456:             )
457: 
458:             # --- Prefill portion (remaining requests) ---
459:             # CRITICAL: use prefill-specific max_seq_len so flash_attn's
460:             # fast path (max_query_len == max_seq_len) triggers for
461:             # first-chunk prefills. Using full-batch max_seq_len breaks
462:             # this because decode requests inflate max_seq_len.
463:             prefill_seq_lens = attn_metadata.seq_lens[num_decodes:]
464:             # Use CPU-side max to avoid GPU→CPU sync from .item()
465:             prefill_max_seq = max(attn_metadata.seq_lens[num_decodes:].tolist())
466:             prefill_qsl = (
467:                 attn_metadata.query_start_loc[num_decodes:] - num_decode_tokens
468:             )
469:             prefill_meta = TurboQuantMetadata(
470:                 seq_lens=prefill_seq_lens,
471:                 slot_mapping=attn_metadata.slot_mapping[num_decode_tokens:N],
472:                 block_table=attn_metadata.block_table[num_decodes:],
473:                 query_start_loc=prefill_qsl,
474:                 num_actual_tokens=N - num_decode_tokens,
475:                 max_query_len=attn_metadata.max_query_len,
476:                 max_seq_len=prefill_max_seq,
477:                 is_prefill=True,
478:             )
479:             k = key[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
480:             v = value[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
481:             attn_out[num_decode_tokens:] = self._prefill_attention(
482:                 q[num_decode_tokens:],
483:                 k[num_decode_tokens:],
484:                 v[num_decode_tokens:],
485:                 kv_cache,
486:                 prefill_meta,
487:                 Pi,
488:                 centroids,
489:                 PiT,
490:                 layer=layer,
491:             )
492: 
493:         # Write into output buffer: attn_out is (N, Hq, D)
494:         # output may be 2D (N, Hq*D) or 3D (N, Hq, D)
495:         if output.ndim == 3:
496:             output[:N] = attn_out.to(output.dtype)
497:         else:
498:             output[:N] = attn_out.reshape(N, -1).to(output.dtype)
499:         return output

557:         # Continuation or no flash_attn: per-request attention.
558:         # For continuation chunks (seq_len > q_len), we must attend to
559:         # previously cached K/V from the TQ cache, not just the current
560:         # chunk's raw K/V.
561:         Hk = key.shape[1]
562:         use_gqa = Hk < Hq
563:         query_start_loc = attn_metadata.query_start_loc
564:         num_reqs = query_start_loc.shape[0] - 1
565: 
566:         output = torch.zeros(N, Hq, D, device=query.device, dtype=query.dtype)
567: 
568:         # Convert to Python lists once (single CPU-GPU sync) instead of

570:         qsl = query_start_loc.tolist()
571:         seq_lens_list = attn_metadata.seq_lens.tolist()
572: 
573:         # Pre-allocate cu_seqlens for single-request flash_attn calls
574:         # to avoid per-request host→device tensor creation.
575:         _cu_2 = torch.zeros(2, device=query.device, dtype=torch.int32)
576: 
577:         for i in range(num_reqs):
578:             q_start = qsl[i]
579:             q_end = qsl[i + 1]
580:             q_len = q_end - q_start
581:             if q_len <= 0:
582:                 continue
583: 
584:             seq_len = seq_lens_list[i]
585:             q_seq = query[q_start:q_end]  # (q_len, Hq, D)
586:             k_seq = key[q_start:q_end]  # (q_len, Hk, D)
587:             v_seq = value[q_start:q_end]  # (q_len, Hk, D)
588: 
589:             if q_len == seq_len:
590:                 # First-chunk prefill: all K/V are in the current batch.
591:                 if _HAS_FLASH_ATTN:
592:                     _cu_2[1] = q_len
593:                     cu = _cu_2
594:                     out = self._flash_attn_varlen(
595:                         q=q_seq,
596:                         k=k_seq,
597:                         v=v_seq,
598:                         cu_seqlens_q=cu,

600:                         max_seqlen_q=q_len,
601:                         max_seqlen_k=q_len,
602:                     )
603:                 else:
604:                     q_t = q_seq.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
605:                     k_t = k_seq.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
606:                     v_t = v_seq.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
607:                     out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
608:                         q_t,
609:                         k_t,
610:                         v_t,
611:                         is_causal=True,
612:                         scale=self.scale,
613:                         enable_gqa=use_gqa,
614:                     ).transpose(0, 1)
615:                 output[q_start:q_end] = out.to(query.dtype)
616:             else:
617:                 # Continuation chunk: tokens already stored to TQ cache
618:                 # by do_kv_cache_update. Use decode kernel directly to
619:                 # avoid O(cached_len) full-dequant per continuation.
620:                 # For large continuations, fall back to _continuation_prefill.
621:                 cached_len = seq_len - q_len
622:                 if q_len <= _CONTINUATION_DECODE_THRESHOLD:
623:                     # Fast path: treat each query as a decode request

768:                 k=k_full,
769:                 v=v_full,
770:                 cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens_q,
771:                 cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens_k,
772:                 max_seqlen_q=q_len,
773:                 max_seqlen_k=seq_len,
774:             )
775:         else:
776:             # SDPA fallback: expand KV for GQA, build causal mask
777:             q_t = query.transpose(0, 1).unsqueeze(0)  # (1, Hq, q_len, D)
778:             k_t = k_full.transpose(0, 1).unsqueeze(0)  # (1, Hk, seq_len, D)
779:             v_t = v_full.transpose(0, 1).unsqueeze(0)  # (1, Hk, seq_len, D)
780:             # Build causal mask: query position p can attend to K position j
781:             # where j <= cached_len + p (p is 0-indexed within chunk)
782:             q_pos = torch.arange(q_len, device=device).unsqueeze(1) + cached_len
783:             k_pos = torch.arange(seq_len, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
784:             mask = k_pos <= q_pos  # (q_len, seq_len)
785:             out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
786:                 q_t,
787:                 k_t,
788:                 v_t,
789:                 attn_mask=mask,
790:                 scale=self.scale,
791:                 enable_gqa=(Hk < Hq),
792:             )  # (1, Hq, q_len, D)
793:             return out[0].transpose(0, 1)  # (q_len, Hq, D)
794: 
795:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
796:     #  Decode: Triton TQ decode attention                                 #
797:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
798:     def _decode_attention(
799:         self,
800:         query: torch.Tensor,  # (B, Hq, D)
801:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,  # (num_blocks, block_size, Hk, slot_size)
802:         attn_metadata: TurboQuantMetadata,
803:         Pi: torch.Tensor,
804:         centroids: torch.Tensor,
805:         PiT: torch.Tensor | None = None,
806:         layer: torch.nn.Module | None = None,
807:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
808:         # Grab cached decode buffers from the layer (lazily allocated).
809:         mid_o_buf = output_buf = lse_buf = None
810:         if layer is not None:
811:             mid_o_buf = getattr(layer, "_tq_mid_o_buf", None)
812:             output_buf = getattr(layer, "_tq_output_buf", None)
813:             lse_buf = getattr(layer, "_tq_lse_buf", None)
814: 
815:         result = triton_turboquant_decode_attention(
816:             query=query,
817:             kv_cache=kv_cache,
818:             block_table=attn_metadata.block_table,
819:             seq_lens=attn_metadata.seq_lens,
820:             Pi=Pi,
821:             centroids=centroids,
822:             scale=self.scale,
823:             mse_bits=self.tq_config.key_mse_bits,
824:             key_packed_size=self.tq_config.key_packed_size,
825:             value_quant_bits=self.tq_config.effective_value_quant_bits,
826:             key_fp8=self.tq_config.key_fp8,
827:             norm_correction=self.tq_config.norm_correction,
828:             PiT=PiT,
829:             mid_o_buf=mid_o_buf,
830:             output_buf=output_buf,
831:             lse_buf=lse_buf,
832:             buf_holder=layer,
833:             max_num_kv_splits=self.max_num_kv_splits,
834:         )
835:         return result

(End of file - total 835 lines)
</content>

1: import torch
2: from vllm.config import get_current_vllm_config
3: from vllm.distributed import get_tensor_model_parallel_rank, get_tensor_model_parallel_world_size

8: from .base import AscendAttentionScheme
9: from .registry import register_scheme

11: 
12: def _fa_quant_weight_loader(param: torch.Tensor, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor):
13:     """Weight loader for MLA-based C8 (FAKQuant) models."""
14:     if param.numel() == 1 and loaded_weight.numel() == 1:
15:         param.data.fill_(loaded_weight.item())
16:     else:
17:         tp_rank = get_tensor_model_parallel_rank()
18:         tp_size = get_tensor_model_parallel_world_size()
19:         shard_size = loaded_weight.shape[0] // tp_size
20:         loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(0, shard_size * tp_rank, shard_size)
21:         assert param.size() == loaded_weight.size(), (
22:             "[vllm-ascend/FAKQuant] Attempted to load weight "
23:             f"({loaded_weight.size()}) into parameter ({param.size()}) "
24:             f"when TP size is {tp_size} and TP rank is {tp_rank}."
25:         )
26: 
27:         param.data.copy_(loaded_weight)
28: 
29: 
30: @register_scheme("FAKQuant", "attention")
31: class AscendFAQuantAttentionMethod:
32:     def __init__(self):
33:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
34:         config = vllm_config.model_config.hf_config
35:         self.kv_lora_rank = getattr(config, "kv_lora_rank", 0)
36:         self.qk_rope_head_dim = getattr(config, "qk_rope_head_dim", 0)
37: 
38:     def create_weights(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
39:         extra_module_names = ["fa_q", "fa_k", "fa_v"]
40:         for name in extra_module_names:
41:             setattr(layer, name, torch.nn.Module())
42:         params_dict = {}
43:         dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()
44:         params_dict["fa_q.scale"] = torch.empty((layer.num_heads, 1), dtype=dtype)
45:         params_dict["fa_k.scale"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=dtype)
46:         params_dict["fa_v.scale"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=dtype)
47:         params_dict["fa_q.offset"] = torch.empty((layer.num_heads, 1), dtype=torch.int8)
48:         params_dict["fa_k.offset"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=torch.int8)
49:         params_dict["fa_v.offset"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=torch.int8)
50: 
51:         for name, weight in params_dict.items():
52:             module_name, weight_name = name.rsplit(".", 1)
53:             module = getattr(layer, module_name)
54:             weight_param = torch.nn.Parameter(weight, requires_grad=False)
55:             module.register_parameter(weight_name, weight_param)
56:             # When loading weights, segment them according to TP
57:             weight_param.weight_loader = _fa_quant_weight_loader
58: 
59:     def process_weights_after_loading(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
60:         fa_k_scale = torch.squeeze(layer.fa_k.scale).unsqueeze(0)
61:         layer.fak_descale_float = torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale.to(torch.float), requires_grad=False)
62:         layer.fak_descale = torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale, requires_grad=False)
63:         if get_ascend_device_type() == AscendDeviceType.A5:
64:             layer.fak_descale_reciprocal = 1.0 / torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale.to(torch.float), requires_grad=False)
65:         else:
66:             layer.fak_descale_reciprocal = 1.0 / torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale, requires_grad=False)
67:         fa_k_offset = torch.squeeze(layer.fa_k.offset).unsqueeze(0)
68:         layer.fak_offset = torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_offset.to(layer.fak_descale.dtype), requires_grad=False)
69: 
70:         repeated_quant_kscale = fa_k_scale.repeat(self.kv_lora_rank)
71:         layer.quant_kscale = repeated_quant_kscale.view(1, self.kv_lora_rank)
72:         layer.quant_kscale = 1.0 / torch.nn.Parameter(layer.quant_kscale.to(torch.float), requires_grad=False)
73: 
74:

78:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
79:         config = vllm_config.model_config.hf_config
80:         self.index_head_dim = config.index_head_dim

88:         params_dict["indexer.k_rot"] = torch.empty((self.index_head_dim, self.index_head_dim), dtype=torch.float32)

91:             module = getattr(layer, module_name)

93:             module.register_parameter(weight_name, weight_param)
94:

96:         pass
97: 
98: 
99: def _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader(param: torch.nn.Parameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor) -> None:
100:     """Weight loader for dense-attention C8 KV cache scales/offsets."""
101:     loaded_weight = loaded_weight.squeeze()
102:     if param.data.shape != loaded_weight.shape:
103:         param.data = loaded_weight.to(param.dtype).clone()
104:     else:
105:         param.data.copy_(loaded_weight)
106: 
107: 
108: class AscendC8KVCacheAttentionMethod(AscendAttentionScheme):
109:     """C8 INT8 KV cache quantization for dense-attention models (e.g. Qwen3)."""
110: 
111:     def __init__(self, quant_description: dict, prefix: str):
112:         self.quant_description = quant_description
113:         self.prefix = prefix
114:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
115:         self.is_kv_producer = False
116:         if vllm_config.kv_transfer_config is not None:
117:             self.is_kv_producer = vllm_config.kv_transfer_config.is_kv_producer
118: 
119:     def create_weights(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
120:         # Returns int8 if the P node is not a PD detachment node.
121:         if not self.is_kv_producer:
122:             logger.info_once(
123:                 "[vllm-ascend/C8_KV] KV cache producer is disabled; setting kv_cache_torch_dtype to torch.int8."
124:             )
125:             layer.kv_cache_torch_dtype = torch.int8
126:         # Upgrade impl to the C8-specific subclass so the C8 forward path is always used.
127:         if hasattr(layer, "impl"):
128:             from vllm_ascend.attention.attention_v1 import AscendC8AttentionBackendImpl
129: 
130:             layer.impl.__class__ = AscendC8AttentionBackendImpl
131:         dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()
132:         layer.k_cache_scale = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.ones(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
133:         layer.k_cache_scale.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
134:         layer.k_cache_offset = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
135:         layer.k_cache_offset.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
136:         layer.v_cache_scale = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.ones(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
137:         layer.v_cache_scale.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
138:         layer.v_cache_offset = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
139:         layer.v_cache_offset.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
140: 
141:     def process_weights_after_loading(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
142:         layer.k_cache_scale.data = layer.k_cache_scale.data.flatten()
143:         layer.k_cache_offset.data = layer.k_cache_offset.data.flatten()
144:         layer.v_cache_scale.data = layer.v_cache_scale.data.flatten()
145:         layer.v_cache_offset.data = layer.v_cache_offset.data.flatten()
146: 
147:     def apply(
148:         self,
149:         layer: torch.nn.Module,
150:         query: torch.Tensor,
151:         key: torch.Tensor,
152:         value: torch.Tensor,
153:         kv_cache,
154:         attn_metadata,
155:         attn_type,
156:         scale,
157:         output,
158:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
159:         err_msg = (
160:             "[vllm-ascend/C8_KV] AscendC8KVCacheAttentionMethod.apply should "
161:             "not be called. C8 KV cache quantization is handled by the "
162:             "attention backend."
163:         )
164:         raise RuntimeError(err_msg)

# vLLM Ascend Development Guidelines

This document provides instructions for contributors to the vLLM Ascend project. Please read and follow these guidelines to ensure code quality, maintainability, and consistency.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Setup and Environment](#setup-and-environment)
    - [Environment Variables](#environment-variables)
    - [Environment Variable Review Requirement](#environment-variable-review-requirement)
- [Testing](#testing)
    - [Unit and System Tests](#unit-and-system-tests)
    - [Running Tests](#running-tests)
- [Code Style](#code-style)
    - [Python Conventions](#python-conventions)
    - [Naming Conventions](#naming-conventions)
- [NPU-Specific Considerations](#npu-specific-considerations)
    - [Tensor item() Operations](#tensor-item-operations)
    - [Memory and Performance](#memory-and-performance)
- [Model and Plugin Architecture](#model-and-plugin-architecture)
    - [vLLM Ascend Plugin Architecture](#vllm-ascend-plugin-architecture)
    - [Patching Requirement](#patching-requirement)
    - [Model Runner Changes](#model-runner-changes)
- [Commit Messages and Pull Requests](#commit-messages-and-pull-requests)
    - [Commit Message Format](#commit-message-format)
- [Review Checklist](#review-checklist)
    - [Code Quality](#code-quality)
    - [Testing](#testing-1)
    - [Documentation](#documentation)
    - [NPU Considerations](#npu-considerations)
    - [Commit and PR](#commit-and-pr)
- [Quick Start for Contributors](#quick-start-for-contributors)
- [References](#references)

---

## Setup and Environment

### Environment Variables

All environment variables must be defined in `vllm_ascend/envs.py` using the centralized `env_variables` dictionary.

**Requirements:**

- Add documentation for each environment variable in the `env_variables` dict comment
- Specify default values and valid ranges
- Indicate whether the variable is sensitive (credentials, keys)

**Example:**

```python
import os

env_variables = {
    "VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_NZ": lambda: int(os.getenv("VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_NZ", 1)),
    # ...
}
```

**Never**: Hardcode environment variable names throughout the codebase. Reference them from the central module using `from vllm_ascend import envs`.

### Environment Variable Review Requirement

**Strict Review Required**: All new environment variables must undergo code review.

Reviewers must verify:

- The variable name follows the `VLLM_ASCEND_*` naming convention
- Default value is appropriate for all supported hardware
- Documentation is added to the `env_variables` dict
- The variable is used in a performance-critical path

---

## Testing

### Unit and System Tests

**Requirement**: All new functionality requires corresponding tests.

- **Unit Tests (UT)**: Located in `tests/ut/`, cover core logic, edge cases, and error conditions
- **System Tests (ST)**: Located in `tests/e2e/`, verify end-to-end behavior and integration points
- **Nightly Tests**: Include benchmarks for NPU-specific code paths in `tests/e2e/nightly/`

**Test Coverage Guidelines:**

- New features: Tests must cover happy path and failure modes
- Bug fixes: Tests must include a regression test for the bug
- Performance-critical code: Include benchmarks and performance regression tests

### Running Tests

```bash


# Run specific unit test file
pytest -sv tests/ut/ops/test_prepare_finalize.py

# Run specific unit test
pytest -sv tests/ut/ops/test_prepare_finalize.py::test_prepare_inputs

# Run NPU-specific tests (requires NPU hardware)
pytest -sv tests/e2e/singlecard/test_piecewise_res_consistency.py
```

**Requirement**: Run all tests locally before requesting review. Verify tests pass on NPU hardware for NPU-specific changes.

---

## Code Style

### Python Conventions

- **Imports**: All imports at the top of the file. Valid exceptions:
    - Circular imports (use inline imports)
    - Lazy loading for worker/isolation processes
    - Type-checking imports wrapped in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`

- **Global Variables**: Avoid new global variables. Pass dependencies explicitly through function parameters.

    **Allowed:**
    - Constants named `ALL_UPPER_CASE` (e.g., `MAX_BATCH_SIZE` in `envs.py`)
    - Immutable configuration objects

    **Requires Approval:**
    - Any new mutable global state

- **No Magic Numbers**: Use named constants with descriptive names:

    ```python
    # Bad
    if seq_len > 2048: ...

    # Good
    MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH = 2048
    if seq_len > MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH: ...
    ```

- **Descriptive Naming**: Use names that describe functionality, not implementation details.

    ```python
    # Bad
    is_deepseek_v3_r1
    flag1
    tmp_var

    # Good
    supports_dynamic_temperature
    uses_speculative_decoding
    ```

### Naming Conventions

- **Classes**: `PascalCase` (e.g., `NPUModelRunner`, `AscendSampler`, `ACLGraphManager`)
- **Functions/Methods**: `snake_case` (e.g., `forward_pass`, `compute_attention`)
- **Constants**: `ALL_UPPER_CASE` (e.g., `MAX_BATCH_SIZE`, `VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_NZ`)
- **Variables**: `snake_case` (e.g., `token_ids`, `sequence_lengths`)

---

## NPU-Specific Considerations

### Tensor item() Operations

**Warning**: `tensor.item()` operations cause synchronization overhead on NPU when the `tensor` is on device.

If the `tensor` is a device tensor, calling `item()` will triggers a synchronous data transfer from NPU to CPU. This can severely degrade performance in hot paths, causing `AsyncScheduler` to block here.

**Review Requirements:**

1. Profile performance impact before merging
2. Consider alternative patterns:
    - Keep values on device when possible
    - Batch operations to reduce sync frequency
    - Use device-side operations (e.g., `torch.argmax`, `torch.sum`)
3. Document when `item()` is unavoidable (e.g., logging, conditional logic)

**Example Patterns:**

```python
# Bad: In hot loop - causes sync per iteration
for tensor in tensors:
    value = tensor.item()

# Better: Batch operations - single sync
values = [t.item() for t in tensors]  # Single batch sync

# Good: Keep on device when possible
max_value = torch.max(tensor)  # No sync needed
if max_value > threshold:  # Comparison can stay on device
    ...
```

### Memory and Performance

Additional NPU-specific best practices:

- Avoid CPU-NPU memory transfers in hot paths
- Prefer in-place operations where safe (e.g., `x.add_()`, `x.mul_()`)
- Monitor memory fragmentation, especially for long-running processes
- Test with realistic workloads on actual NPU hardware (Ascend 910B/C)

---

## Model and Plugin Architecture

### vLLM Ascend Plugin Architecture

vLLM Ascend is a **hardware plugin** that integrates with upstream vLLM via the pluggable hardware interface. It does not add new model files directly.

**Required Pattern**: Model-specific functionality should be implemented via:

1. **Patching** (in `vllm_ascend/patch/`):
    - `vllm_ascend/patch/platform/` - Platform-level patches (distributed, scheduling)
    - `vllm_ascend/patch/worker/` - Worker-level patches (model-specific behavior)
    - Example: `patch_deepseek.py` modifies upstream Deepseek model behavior
    - Patch is not the best solution for all cases. Use it when necessary.

2. **Inheritance**:
    - `NPUModelRunner(GPUModelRunner)` - Extend vLLM model runner with NPU-specific behavior
    - `AscendSampler` - Extend vLLM sampler with NPU-specific operations
    - Add NPU-specific components via composition (e.g., `AclGraphManager`)
    - Custom Operators - NPU-specific custom operators (e.g., `AscendRMSNorm`)

3. **External upstream contributions** where appropriate

### Patching Requirement

**Strict Review Required**: All new patches must undergo thorough architectural review.

Reviewers must verify:

- The patch targets the correct upstream component
- The patch is minimal and focused
- Performance implications are understood
- A long-term plan exists for upstream contribution

**Example Patch Pattern:**

```python
# vllm_ascend/patch/worker/patch_deepseek.py
from vllm.model_executor.models.deepseek_v2 import DeepseekV2Model

def forward(self, input_ids, positions, ...):
    # NPU-specific forward implementation
    ...

DeepseekV2Model.forward = forward  # Patch upstream class
```

### Model Runner Changes

**Strict Review Required**: All new behaviors added to `model_runner` must undergo thorough architectural review.

Reviewers must verify:

- The necessity of the new behavior (why can't this be in a patch?)
- Performance implications on NPU hardware
- Compatibility with existing model implementations
- Long-term maintainability and test coverage

**NPU Model Runner Files:**

- `vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py` - vLLM v1 model runner
- `vllm_ascend/worker/v2/model_runner.py` - vLLM v2 model runner
- `vllm_ascend/_310p/model_runner_310p.py` - Ascend 310P model runner

---

## Commit Messages and Pull Requests

### Commit Message Format

Follow the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format and **must include a sign-off**:

```bash
git commit -s -m "<type>: <summary>" -m "<body - explaining what changed and why>"
```

Or using the full message format:

```txt
<type>: <summary>

<body - explaining what changed and why>

Signed-off-by: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
```

**Valid Types**: `feat`, `fix`, `perf`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `chore`

**Good Examples:**

```txt
feat(npu): add flash attention support for Ascend CANN

- Implements FlashAttention-2 kernel for NPU backend
- Reduces memory usage by 30% compared to baseline

fix(model_runner): correct padding token handling

- Fixes token padding that caused incorrect attention masks
- Addresses issue #1234

perf: avoid CPU-NPU sync in attention computation

- Inline computation to avoid tensor.item() calls
- Improves throughput by 15%
```

**Bad Examples:**

```txt
fix bug
add feature
update code
```

### Pull Request Title Format

PR titles should follow the format: `[Type][Module] Description`

- **Type**: The type of change (e.g., `CI`, `Doc`, `BugFix`, `Feat`, `Platform`, `Refactor`)
- **Module**: The affected module (optional, e.g., `Misc`, `Model`, `Worker`)
- **Description**: Brief description of the change

**Examples:**

- `[Doc][Misc] Update contribution guidelines`
- `[BugFix] Fix CPU binding logic`
- `[CI] Update image build workflow`

### Pull Request Template

When creating a PR, please follow the template in `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` and ensure the following sections are completed:

> **Note**: The PR description will be automatically updated by GitHub Actions to include vLLM version info at the bottom. If you update the PR description via API or CLI, make sure to preserve the `- vLLM version:` and `- vLLM main:` lines.

- **What this PR does / why we need it?** - Clearly describe the changes and their purpose
- **Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?** - Indicate if there are any user-visible changes
- **How was this patch tested?** - Describe how you tested the changes. Examples:
    - Unit tests added/updated: list the test files
    - Manual testing: provide the test steps and commands
    - CI testing: indicate if only CI verification is needed

---

## Review Checklist

Before merging, verify:

### Code Quality

- [ ] Code follows style guidelines (naming, imports, no magic numbers)
- [ ] No global state added without justification
- [ ] Patching pattern used correctly (if applicable)
- [ ] No direct model file additions

### Testing

- [ ] New tests added for new functionality (`tests/ut/` or `tests/e2e/`)
- [ ] Existing tests pass
- [ ] NPU-specific tests verified on actual hardware
- [ ] Performance benchmarks included where applicable

### Documentation

- [ ] Environment variables documented
- [ ] Public APIs documented
- [ ] User-facing changes reflected in docs

### NPU Considerations

- [ ] `tensor.item()` usage reviewed for performance impact
- [ ] No unnecessary CPU-NPU transfers in hot paths
- [ ] Memory usage verified on NPU hardware

### Commit and PR

- [ ] Commit messages are clear and descriptive, following Conventional Commits format
- [ ] **All commits are signed off** (`git commit -s`)
- [ ] PR is created from your fork repository, not directly from the main repository
- [ ] PR description is complete, following the PR template
- [ ] All review comments addressed

---

## Quick Start for Contributors

1. Install development dependencies: `pip install -e .[dev]`
2. Run tests: `pytest tests/`
3. Check linting: `ruff check vllm_ascend/`
4. Format code: `ruff format vllm_ascend/`
5. Make your changes following guidelines in this document
6. Add tests for new behavior
7. Run full test suite before committing
8. Commit with sign-off: `git commit -s`
9. Run linting check before pushing:
   ```bash
   bash format.sh ci
   ```
   > **Note**: This check is required for **all file types**, including markdown files. If `markdownlint` modifies files, re-add them with `git add` and commit again.
10. Push to your fork repository (NOT the main repository):

   ```bash
   git remote add myfork https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/vllm-ascend.git
   git push -u myfork your-branch-name
   ```

11. Create a PR from your fork to the main repository with clear description

---

- [Documentation](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/ascend/en/latest/)
- [Contributors Guide](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/ascend/en/latest/community/contributors.html)

</system-reminder>

111:     def swap_blocks(
112:         src_kv_cache: list[torch.Tensor],
113:         dst_kv_cache: list[torch.Tensor],
114:         src_to_dst: torch.Tensor,
115:     ) -> None:
116:         src_key_cache, src_value_cache = src_kv_cache[0], src_kv_cache[1]
117:         dst_key_cache, dst_value_cache = dst_kv_cache[0], dst_kv_cache[1]
118:         src_indices = src_to_dst[:, 0]
119:         dst_indices = src_to_dst[:, 1]
120: 
121:         dst_key_cache[dst_indices] = src_key_cache[src_indices].to(dst_key_cache.device)
122:         dst_value_cache[dst_indices] = src_value_cache[src_indices].to(dst_key_cache.device)
