203:     def build(self, common_prefix_len, common_attn_metadata, fast_build=False):
204:         """Build TurboQuantMetadata from common attention metadata."""
205:         cam = common_attn_metadata
206: 
207:         # With reorder_batch_threshold=1, the model runner guarantees
208:         # decodes come first in the batch. split_decodes_and_prefills
209:         # finds the boundary (operates on CPU tensors — no GPU sync).
210:         assert self.reorder_batch_threshold is not None
211:         num_decodes, num_prefills, num_decode_tokens, _ = split_decodes_and_prefills(
212:             cam, decode_threshold=self.reorder_batch_threshold
213:         )
214: 
215:         return TurboQuantMetadata(
216:             seq_lens=cam.seq_lens,
217:             slot_mapping=cam.slot_mapping,
218:             block_table=cam.block_table_tensor,
219:             query_start_loc=cam.query_start_loc,
220:             num_actual_tokens=cam.num_actual_tokens,
221:             max_query_len=cam.max_query_len,
222:             max_seq_len=cam.max_seq_len,
223:             is_prefill=(cam.max_query_len > 1),
224:             num_decodes=num_decodes,
225:             num_decode_tokens=num_decode_tokens,
226:         )
227: 
228: 
229: class TurboQuantAttentionImpl(AttentionImpl["TurboQuantMetadata"]):
230:     """TurboQuant attention implementation.
231: 
232:     Vectorized PyTorch: batch quantize/store, vectorized bit-unpack
233:     decode with einsum scores and value gather.
234:     """
235: 
236:     supports_quant_query_input: bool = False
237: 
238:     def __init__(
239:         self,
240:         num_heads: int,
241:         head_size: int,
242:         scale: float,
243:         num_kv_heads: int | None = None,
244:         alibi_slopes: list[float] | None = None,
245:         sliding_window: int | None = None,
246:         kv_cache_dtype: str = "auto",
247:         logits_soft_cap: float | None = None,
248:         attn_type: str = AttentionType.DECODER,
249:         kv_sharing_target_layer_name: str | None = None,
250:         **kwargs,
251:     ):
252:         self.num_heads = num_heads
253:         self.head_size = head_size
254:         self.scale = scale
255:         self.num_kv_heads = num_kv_heads if num_kv_heads is not None else num_heads
256:         self.num_kv_groups = num_heads // self.num_kv_heads
257:         self.kv_cache_dtype = kv_cache_dtype
258: 
259:         from vllm.model_executor.layers.quantization.turboquant.config import (
260:             TurboQuantConfig,
261:         )
262: 
263:         self.tq_config = TurboQuantConfig.from_cache_dtype(kv_cache_dtype, head_size)
264: 
265:         # Pre-compute kernel constants from config (avoid repeated arithmetic)
266:         cfg = self.tq_config
267:         self._mse_bytes = (
268:             math.ceil(head_size * cfg.key_mse_bits / 8)
269:             if not cfg.key_fp8
270:             else head_size
271:         )
272:         self._val_data_bytes = math.ceil(head_size * cfg.effective_value_quant_bits / 8)
273:         self._n_centroids = cfg.n_centroids if not cfg.key_fp8 else 1
274: 
275:         # Detect flash-attn version (FA2/3/4) for prefill paths.
276:         self.fa_version = get_flash_attn_version(head_size=head_size)
277: 
278:         # Fixed NUM_KV_SPLITS (grid dims must be constant for cudagraph,
279:         # and benchmarks show no regression vs dynamic in eager mode).
280:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
281:         self.max_num_kv_splits = (
282:             vllm_config.attention_config.tq_max_kv_splits_for_cuda_graph
283:         )
284: 
285:     def _flash_attn_varlen(
286:         self,
287:         q: torch.Tensor,
288:         k: torch.Tensor,
289:         v: torch.Tensor,
290:         cu_seqlens_q: torch.Tensor,
291:         cu_seqlens_k: torch.Tensor,
292:         max_seqlen_q: int,
293:         max_seqlen_k: int,
294:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
295:         # fa_utils.get_flash_attn_version() returns None on backends that
296:         # should not pass an explicit fa_version kwarg.
297:         if self.fa_version is None:
298:             return flash_attn_varlen_func(
299:                 q=q,
300:                 k=k,
301:                 v=v,
302:                 cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens_q,
303:                 cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens_k,
304:                 max_seqlen_q=max_seqlen_q,
305:                 max_seqlen_k=max_seqlen_k,
306:                 softmax_scale=self.scale,
307:                 causal=True,
308:             )
309:         return flash_attn_varlen_func(
310:             q=q,
311:             k=k,
312:             v=v,
313:             cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens_q,
314:             cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens_k,
315:             max_seqlen_q=max_seqlen_q,
316:             max_seqlen_k=max_seqlen_k,
317:             softmax_scale=self.scale,
318:             causal=True,
319:             fa_version=self.fa_version,
320:         )
321: 
322:     def _ensure_on_device(self, layer, device):
323:         """One-time derivation of TQ buffers (rotation matrix, midpoints).
324: 
325:         The Hadamard rotation is shared across all layers: random sign
326:         flips do not improve Lloyd-Max quantization quality because the
327:         quantizer is symmetric around zero (sign-flipping a coordinate
328:         maps it to the mirror centroid with identical distortion).
329:         """
330:         if not hasattr(layer, "_tq_cached"):
331:             D = self.head_size
332: 
333:             # Pure Hadamard: orthonormal + symmetric (H = H^T), enabling
334:             # in-kernel butterfly fusion and trivial inverse for continuation.
335:             H = _build_hadamard(D, str(device))
336:             layer._tq_PiT = H
337:             layer._tq_Pi = H
338: 
339:             c = layer._tq_centroids.to(device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
340:             c_sorted, _ = c.sort()
341:             layer._tq_midpoints = (c_sorted[:-1] + c_sorted[1:]) / 2
342:             layer._tq_cached = True
343: 
344:     def do_kv_cache_update(
345:         self,
346:         layer: torch.nn.Module,
347:         key: torch.Tensor,
348:         value: torch.Tensor,
349:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
350:         slot_mapping: torch.Tensor,
351:     ) -> None:
352:         """Store compressed K/V into the combined TQ cache.
353: 
354:         Called as a separate custom op (unified_kv_cache_update) BEFORE
355:         the attention forward, matching FlashAttention's split pattern.
356:         slot_mapping is already sliced to num_actual_tokens by the caller.
357:         """
358:         N = slot_mapping.shape[0]
359:         if N <= 0:
360:             return
361: 
362:         device = key.device
363:         self._ensure_on_device(layer, device)
364: 
365:         k = key[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
366:         v = value[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
367:         self._store_kv(k, v, kv_cache, slot_mapping, layer)
368: 
369:     def forward(
370:         self,
371:         layer: AttentionLayer,
372:         query: torch.Tensor,
373:         key: torch.Tensor,
374:         value: torch.Tensor,
375:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
376:         attn_metadata: "TurboQuantMetadata",
377:         output: torch.Tensor | None = None,
378:         output_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
379:         output_block_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
380:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
381:         num_tokens = query.shape[0]
382: 
383:         if output is None:
384:             output = torch.zeros(
385:                 num_tokens,
386:                 self.num_heads * self.head_size,
387:                 dtype=query.dtype,
388:                 device=query.device,
389:             )
390: 
391:         if attn_metadata is None:
392:             return output.fill_(0)
393: 
394:         # Slice to actual tokens
395:         N = attn_metadata.num_actual_tokens
396:         if N <= 0:
397:             return output.fill_(0)
398: 
399:         q = query[:N].view(N, self.num_heads, self.head_size)
400: 
401:         # Get TQ buffers, ensure on device (one-time migration).
402:         # Use Any-typed alias for dynamic _tq_* attrs set by _ensure_on_device.
403:         tq_layer: Any = layer
404:         device = q.device
405:         self._ensure_on_device(tq_layer, device)
406:         Pi = tq_layer._tq_Pi
407:         PiT = tq_layer._tq_PiT
408:         centroids = tq_layer._tq_centroids
409: 
410:         # Compute attention (KV cache was already updated by do_kv_cache_update)
411:         # With reorder_batch_threshold=1, decodes come first in the batch.
412:         # num_decodes/num_decode_tokens from metadata give the split point.
413:         num_decodes = attn_metadata.num_decodes
414:         num_decode_tokens = attn_metadata.num_decode_tokens
415: 
416:         if not attn_metadata.is_prefill:
417:             # Pure decode batch — fast path
418:             attn_out = self._decode_attention(
419:                 q, kv_cache, attn_metadata, Pi, centroids, PiT, layer
420:             )
421:         elif num_decodes == 0:
422:             # Pure prefill batch
423:             k = key[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
424:             v = value[:N].view(N, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
425:             attn_out = self._prefill_attention(
426:                 q,
427:                 k,
428:                 v,
429:                 kv_cache,
430:                 attn_metadata,
431:                 Pi,
432:                 centroids,
433:                 PiT,
434:                 layer=layer,
435:             )
436:         else:
437:             # Mixed batch: decodes first (guaranteed by reorder_batch).
438:             attn_out = torch.zeros(
439:                 N, self.num_heads, self.head_size, device=device, dtype=q.dtype
440:             )
441: 
442:             # --- Decode portion (first num_decodes requests) ---
443:             # Use full-batch max_seq_len as safe upper bound (no GPU sync).
444:             decode_meta = TurboQuantMetadata(
445:                 seq_lens=attn_metadata.seq_lens[:num_decodes],
446:                 slot_mapping=attn_metadata.slot_mapping[:num_decode_tokens],
447:                 block_table=attn_metadata.block_table[:num_decodes],
448:                 query_start_loc=attn_metadata.query_start_loc[: num_decodes + 1],
449:                 num_actual_tokens=num_decode_tokens,

493:         # Write into output buffer: attn_out is (N, Hq, D)
494:         # output may be 2D (N, Hq*D) or 3D (N, Hq, D)

501:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
502:     #  Store K/V into combined cache (vectorized)                         #
503:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
504:     def _store_kv(
505:         self,
506:         key: torch.Tensor,  # (N, Hk, D)
507:         value: torch.Tensor,  # (N, Hk, D)
508:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,  # (num_blocks, block_size, Hk, slot_size)
509:         slot_mapping: torch.Tensor,
510:         layer: Any,
511:     ):
512:         """Quantize + store via fused Triton kernel."""
513:         triton_turboquant_store(
514:             key,
515:             value,
516:             kv_cache,
517:             slot_mapping,
518:             layer._tq_PiT,
519:             layer._tq_midpoints,
520:             mse_bits=self.tq_config.key_mse_bits,
521:             key_packed_size=self.tq_config.key_packed_size,
522:             value_quant_bits=self.tq_config.effective_value_quant_bits,
523:             key_fp8=self.tq_config.key_fp8,
524:         )
525: 
526:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
527:     #  Prefill: SDPA on raw Q/K/V with causal mask                        #
528:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
529:     def _prefill_attention(
530:         self,
531:         query: torch.Tensor,  # (N, Hq, D)
532:         key: torch.Tensor,  # (N, Hk, D)
533:         value: torch.Tensor,  # (N, Hk, D)
534:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,  # (num_blocks, block_size, Hk, slot_size)
535:         attn_metadata: TurboQuantMetadata,
536:         Pi: torch.Tensor,
537:         centroids: torch.Tensor,
538:         PiT: torch.Tensor | None = None,
539:         layer: Any = None,
540:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
541:         N, Hq, D = query.shape
542: 
543:         # Fast path: use flash_attn for first-chunk prefills (all K/V in batch).
544:         # max_query_len == max_seq_len means no request has prior cached KV.
545:         # Both are Python ints — no GPU sync.
546:         if _HAS_FLASH_ATTN and attn_metadata.max_query_len == attn_metadata.max_seq_len:
547:             return self._flash_attn_varlen(
548:                 q=query,
549:                 k=key,
550:                 v=value,
551:                 cu_seqlens_q=attn_metadata.query_start_loc,
552:                 cu_seqlens_k=attn_metadata.query_start_loc,
553:                 max_seqlen_q=attn_metadata.max_query_len,
554:                 max_seqlen_k=attn_metadata.max_query_len,
555:             )
556: 
557:         # Continuation or no flash_attn: per-request attention.
558:         # For continuation chunks (seq_len > q_len), we must attend to
559:         # previously cached K/V from the TQ cache, not just the current
560:         # chunk's raw K/V.
561:         Hk = key.shape[1]
562:         use_gqa = Hk < Hq
563:         query_start_loc = attn_metadata.query_start_loc
564:         num_reqs = query_start_loc.shape[0] - 1
565: 
566:         output = torch.zeros(N, Hq, D, device=query.device, dtype=query.dtype)
567: 
568:         # Convert to Python lists once (single CPU-GPU sync) instead of
569:         # per-request .item() calls that each force a sync.
570:         qsl = query_start_loc.tolist()
571:         seq_lens_list = attn_metadata.seq_lens.tolist()
572: 
573:         # Pre-allocate cu_seqlens for single-request flash_attn calls
574:         # to avoid per-request host→device tensor creation.
575:         _cu_2 = torch.zeros(2, device=query.device, dtype=torch.int32)
576: 
577:         for i in range(num_reqs):
578:             q_start = qsl[i]
579:             q_end = qsl[i + 1]
580:             q_len = q_end - q_start
581:             if q_len <= 0:
582:                 continue
583: 
584:             seq_len = seq_lens_list[i]
585:             q_seq = query[q_start:q_end]  # (q_len, Hq, D)
586:             k_seq = key[q_start:q_end]  # (q_len, Hk, D)
587:             v_seq = value[q_start:q_end]  # (q_len, Hk, D)
588: 
589:             if q_len == seq_len:
590:                 # First-chunk prefill: all K/V are in the current batch.
591:                 if _HAS_FLASH_ATTN:
592:                     _cu_2[1] = q_len
593:                     cu = _cu_2
594:                     out = self._flash_attn_varlen(
595:                         q=q_seq,
596:                         k=k_seq,
597:                         v=v_seq,
598:                         cu_seqlens_q=cu,
599:                         cu_seqlens_k=cu,
600:                         max_seqlen_q=q_len,
601:                         max_seqlen_k=q_len,
602:                     )
603:                 else:
604:                     q_t = q_seq.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
605:                     k_t = k_seq.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
606:                     v_t = v_seq.transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
607:                     out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
608:                         q_t,
609:                         k_t,
610:                         v_t,
611:                         is_causal=True,
612:                         scale=self.scale,
613:                         enable_gqa=use_gqa,
614:                     ).transpose(0, 1)
615:                 output[q_start:q_end] = out.to(query.dtype)
616:             else:
617:                 # Continuation chunk: tokens already stored to TQ cache
618:                 # by do_kv_cache_update. Use decode kernel directly to
619:                 # avoid O(cached_len) full-dequant per continuation.
620:                 # For large continuations, fall back to _continuation_prefill.
621:                 cached_len = seq_len - q_len
622:                 if q_len <= _CONTINUATION_DECODE_THRESHOLD:
623:                     # Fast path: treat each query as a decode request
624:                     # with incremental seq_lens for causal masking.
625:                     synth_seq_lens = torch.arange(
626:                         cached_len + 1,
627:                         seq_len + 1,
628:                         device=query.device,
629:                         dtype=attn_metadata.seq_lens.dtype,
630:                     )
631:                     synth_bt = attn_metadata.block_table[i : i + 1].expand(q_len, -1)
632:                     out = triton_turboquant_decode_attention(
633:                         query=q_seq,
634:                         kv_cache=kv_cache,
635:                         block_table=synth_bt,
636:                         seq_lens=synth_seq_lens,
637:                         Pi=Pi,
638:                         centroids=centroids,
639:                         scale=self.scale,
640:                         mse_bits=self.tq_config.key_mse_bits,
641:                         key_packed_size=self.tq_config.key_packed_size,
642:                         value_quant_bits=(self.tq_config.effective_value_quant_bits),
643:                         key_fp8=self.tq_config.key_fp8,
644:                         norm_correction=self.tq_config.norm_correction,
645:                         PiT=PiT,
646:                     )
647:                 else:
648:                     # Large continuation: dequant cached K/V and use
649:                     # flash_attn for better throughput.
650:                     out = self._continuation_prefill(
651:                         layer,
652:                         q_seq,
653:                         k_seq,
654:                         v_seq,
655:                         kv_cache,
656:                         attn_metadata.block_table[i : i + 1],
657:                         cached_len,
658:                         seq_len,
659:                         Pi,
660:                         centroids,
661:                     )
662:                 output[q_start:q_end] = out.to(query.dtype)
663: 
664:         return output
665: 
666:     def _continuation_prefill(
667:         self,
668:         layer: Any,
669:         query: torch.Tensor,  # (q_len, Hq, D)
670:         key_chunk: torch.Tensor,  # (q_len, Hk, D)
671:         val_chunk: torch.Tensor,  # (q_len, Hk, D)
672:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,  # (num_blocks, block_size, Hk, slot_size)
673:         block_table: torch.Tensor,  # (1, max_num_blocks)
674:         cached_len: int,
675:         seq_len: int,
676:         Pi: torch.Tensor,
677:         centroids: torch.Tensor,
678:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
679:         """Handle continuation chunk by dequanting cached K/V from TQ cache.
680: 
681:         Dequants previously cached K/V, concatenates with the current
682:         chunk's raw K/V, then runs flash_attn with causal masking.
683:         """
684:         q_len, Hq, D = query.shape
685:         Hk = key_chunk.shape[1]
686:         device = query.device
687:         block_size = kv_cache.shape[1]
688:         BLOCK_D = triton.next_power_of_2(D)
689: 
690:         mse_bytes = self._mse_bytes
691:         val_data_bytes = self._val_data_bytes
692: 
693:         # Dequant cached K/V from TQ cache
694:         # Allocate slightly over to align to block_size for the grid.
695:         # Reuse cached buffers to avoid per-call allocation (~16MB at 8K).
696:         alloc_len = math.ceil(cached_len / block_size) * block_size
697:         buf_shape = (1, Hk, alloc_len, D)
698:         k_buf = getattr(layer, "_tq_k_dequant_buf", None)
699:         if k_buf is None or k_buf.shape[2] < alloc_len:
700:             k_buf = torch.empty(buf_shape, dtype=torch.float16, device=device)
701:             v_buf = torch.empty(buf_shape, dtype=torch.float16, device=device)
702:             layer._tq_k_dequant_buf = k_buf
703:             layer._tq_v_dequant_buf = v_buf
704:         else:
705:             v_buf = layer._tq_v_dequant_buf
706:         k_cached = k_buf[:, :, :alloc_len, :].zero_()
707:         v_cached = v_buf[:, :, :alloc_len, :].zero_()
708: 
709:         grid = (alloc_len, 1 * Hk)
710:         _tq_full_dequant_kv[grid](
711:             kv_cache,
712:             block_table,
713:             centroids,
714:             k_cached,
715:             v_cached,
716:             k_cached.stride(0),
717:             k_cached.stride(1),
718:             k_cached.stride(2),
719:             v_cached.stride(0),
720:             v_cached.stride(1),
721:             v_cached.stride(2),
722:             kv_cache.stride(0),
723:             kv_cache.stride(1),
724:             kv_cache.stride(2),
725:             block_table.stride(0),
726:             HEAD_DIM=D,
727:             BLOCK_SIZE=block_size,
728:             NUM_KV_HEADS=Hk,
729:             MSE_BYTES=mse_bytes,
730:             KPS=self.tq_config.key_packed_size,
731:             VQB=self.tq_config.effective_value_quant_bits,
732:             VAL_DATA_BYTES=val_data_bytes,
733:             MSE_BITS=self.tq_config.key_mse_bits,
734:             KEY_FP8=1 if self.tq_config.key_fp8 else 0,
735:             BLOCK_D=BLOCK_D,
736:             NORM_CORRECTION=1 if self.tq_config.norm_correction else 0,
737:             FP8_E4B15=_use_fp8_e4b15(device.index or 0),
738:             num_warps=4,
739:         )
740: 
741:         # Inverse-rotate MSE keys back to original space
742:         if not self.tq_config.key_fp8:
743:             k_flat = k_cached[0, :, :cached_len, :].reshape(-1, D).float()
744:             k_flat = k_flat @ Pi
745:             k_cached_trim = (
746:                 k_flat.to(torch.float16).reshape(Hk, cached_len, D).transpose(0, 1)
747:             )  # (cached_len, Hk, D)
748:         else:
749:             k_cached_trim = (
750:                 k_cached[0, :, :cached_len, :].transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
751:             )  # (cached_len, Hk, D)
752: 
753:         v_cached_trim = (
754:             v_cached[0, :, :cached_len, :].transpose(0, 1).contiguous()
755:         )  # (cached_len, Hk, D)
756: 
757:         # Concatenate cached + current chunk K/V (match query dtype)
758:         qdtype = query.dtype
759:         k_full = torch.cat([k_cached_trim.to(qdtype), key_chunk], dim=0)
760:         v_full = torch.cat([v_cached_trim.to(qdtype), val_chunk], dim=0)
761: 
762:         # Attention: q_len queries attending to seq_len K/V with causal mask
763:         if _HAS_FLASH_ATTN:
764:             cu_seqlens_q = torch.tensor([0, q_len], device=device, dtype=torch.int32)
765:             cu_seqlens_k = torch.tensor([0, seq_len], device=device, dtype=torch.int32)
766:             return self._flash_attn_varlen(
767:                 q=query,
768:                 k=k_full,
769:                 v=v_full,
770:                 cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens_q,
771:                 cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens_k,
772:                 max_seqlen_q=q_len,
773:                 max_seqlen_k=seq_len,
774:             )
775:         else:
776:             # SDPA fallback: expand KV for GQA, build causal mask
777:             q_t = query.transpose(0, 1).unsqueeze(0)  # (1, Hq, q_len, D)
778:             k_t = k_full.transpose(0, 1).unsqueeze(0)  # (1, Hk, seq_len, D)
779:             v_t = v_full.transpose(0, 1).unsqueeze(0)  # (1, Hk, seq_len, D)
780:             # Build causal mask: query position p can attend to K position j
781:             # where j <= cached_len + p (p is 0-indexed within chunk)
782:             q_pos = torch.arange(q_len, device=device).unsqueeze(1) + cached_len
783:             k_pos = torch.arange(seq_len, device=device).unsqueeze(0)
784:             mask = k_pos <= q_pos  # (q_len, seq_len)
785:             out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
786:                 q_t,
787:                 k_t,
788:                 v_t,
789:                 attn_mask=mask,
790:                 scale=self.scale,
791:                 enable_gqa=(Hk < Hq),
792:             )  # (1, Hq, q_len, D)
793:             return out[0].transpose(0, 1)  # (q_len, Hq, D)
794: 
795:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
796:     #  Decode: Triton TQ decode attention                                 #
797:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
798:     def _decode_attention(
799:         self,
800:         query: torch.Tensor,  # (B, Hq, D)
801:         kv_cache: torch.Tensor,  # (num_blocks, block_size, Hk, slot_size)
802:         attn_metadata: TurboQuantMetadata,
803:         Pi: torch.Tensor,
804:         centroids: torch.Tensor,
805:         PiT: torch.Tensor | None = None,
806:         layer: torch.nn.Module | None = None,
807:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
808:         # Grab cached decode buffers from the layer (lazily allocated).
809:         mid_o_buf = output_buf = lse_buf = None
810:         if layer is not None:
811:             mid_o_buf = getattr(layer, "_tq_mid_o_buf", None)
812:             output_buf = getattr(layer, "_tq_output_buf", None)
813:             lse_buf = getattr(layer, "_tq_lse_buf", None)
814: 
815:         result = triton_turboquant_decode_attention(
816:             query=query,
817:             kv_cache=kv_cache,
818:             block_table=attn_metadata.block_table,
819:             seq_lens=attn_metadata.seq_lens,
820:             Pi=Pi,
821:             centroids=centroids,
822:             scale=self.scale,
823:             mse_bits=self.tq_config.key_mse_bits,
824:             key_packed_size=self.tq_config.key_packed_size,
825:             value_quant_bits=self.tq_config.effective_value_quant_bits,
826:             key_fp8=self.tq_config.key_fp8,
827:             norm_correction=self.tq_config.norm_correction,
828:             PiT=PiT,
829:             mid_o_buf=mid_o_buf,
830:             output_buf=output_buf,
831:             lse_buf=lse_buf,
832:             buf_holder=layer,
833:             max_num_kv_splits=self.max_num_kv_splits,
834:         )
835:         return result

(End of file - total 835 lines)
</content>

1343: class AscendC8AttentionBackendImpl(AscendAttentionBackendImpl):
1344:     """Attention backend implementation for INT8 KV cache (C8/QuaRot) models.
1345: 
1346:     This subclass handles static per-channel INT8 KV cache quantization.
1347:     It is activated via class surgery in AscendC8KVCacheAttentionMethod.create_weights
1348:     (vllm_ascend/quantization/methods/kv_c8.py)
1349:     so that C8 attention layers automatically use this forward path.
1350:     """
1351: 
1352:     def forward(
1353:         self,
1354:         layer: AttentionLayer,
1355:         query: torch.Tensor,
1356:         key: torch.Tensor,
1357:         value: torch.Tensor,
1358:         kv_cache: tuple[torch.Tensor],
1359:         attn_metadata: AscendMetadata,
1360:         output: torch.Tensor | None = None,
1361:         output_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
1362:         output_block_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
1363:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
1364:         assert output is not None, "Output tensor must be provided."
1365: 
1366:         if output_scale is not None or output_block_scale is not None:
1367:             raise NotImplementedError("fused output quantization is not yet supported for AscendC8AttentionBackendImpl")
1368: 
1369:         num_tokens = query.shape[0]
1370:         if attn_metadata is None:
1371:             return output.fill_(0)
1372: 
1373:         self._prepare_c8_scales(layer, query.device)
1374:         float_key, float_value = None, None
1375:         if self.vllm_config.kv_transfer_config is None:
1376:             if key is not None and value is not None:
1377:                 if attn_metadata.attn_state != AscendAttentionState.DecodeOnly:
1378:                     float_key, float_value = key, value
1379:                 key, value = self._quantize_kv_to_int8(key, value, layer, attn_metadata.num_actual_tokens)
1380:                 query, key, value, _ = self.reshape_and_cache(query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, output)
1381:             # pooling model branch
1382:             if attn_metadata.model_runner_type == "pooling":
1383:                 attn_output = self._forward_encoder_attention(query, key, value, attn_metadata, output)
1384:                 output[:num_tokens] = attn_output[:num_tokens]
1385:                 return output
1386:             if attn_metadata.attn_state == AscendAttentionState.DecodeOnly:
1387:                 if _EXTRA_CTX.capturing:
1388:                     attn_output, num_tokens = self.full_graph_fia(query, key, value, attn_metadata, output, layer)
1389:                     output[:num_tokens] = attn_output[:num_tokens]
1390:                     return output
1391:                 return self._forward_c8_decode(query, attn_metadata, output, layer)
1392:             elif attn_metadata.attn_state == AscendAttentionState.ChunkedPrefill:
1393:                 return self._forward_c8_chunked_prefill(query, float_key, float_value, attn_metadata, output, layer)
1394:             else:
1395:                 return self._forward_c8_fused_infer_attention(
1396:                     query,
1397:                     float_key if float_key is not None else key,
1398:                     float_value if float_value is not None else value,
1399:                     attn_metadata,
1400:                     output,
1401:                     layer,
1402:                 )
1403:         else:
1404:             if attn_metadata.attn_state != AscendAttentionState.DecodeOnly and self.is_kv_producer:
1405:                 output_padded = None
1406:                 if key is not None and value is not None:
1407:                     output_padded = output
1408:                     query, key, value, output_padded = self.reshape_and_cache(
1409:                         query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, output
1410:                     )
1411:                 # pooling model branch
1412:                 if attn_metadata.model_runner_type == "pooling":
1413:                     attn_output = self._forward_encoder_attention(query, key, value, attn_metadata, output)
1414:                     output[:num_tokens] = attn_output[:num_tokens]
1415:                     return output
1416:                 if output_padded is not None:
1417:                     attn_output = self.forward_impl(query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, output_padded)
1418:                 else:
1419:                     attn_output = self.forward_impl(query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, output)
1420:                 output[:num_tokens] = attn_output[:num_tokens]
1421:                 return output
1422:             elif not self.is_kv_producer:
1423:                 if key is not None and value is not None:
1424:                     key, value = self._quantize_kv_to_int8(key, value, layer, attn_metadata.num_actual_tokens)
1425:                     query, key, value, _ = self.reshape_and_cache(query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, output)
1426:                 # pooling model branch
1427:                 if attn_metadata.model_runner_type == "pooling":
1428:                     attn_output = self._forward_encoder_attention(query, key, value, attn_metadata, output)
1429:                     output[:num_tokens] = attn_output[:num_tokens]
1430:                     return output
1431:                 if _EXTRA_CTX.capturing:
1432:                     attn_output, num_tokens = self.full_graph_fia(query, key, value, attn_metadata, output, layer)
1433:                     output[:num_tokens] = attn_output[:num_tokens]
1434:                     return output
1435:                 elif attn_metadata.attn_state == AscendAttentionState.DecodeOnly:
1436:                     return self._forward_c8_decode(query, attn_metadata, output, layer)
1437: 
1438:     def _prepare_c8_scales(self, layer: AttentionLayer, device: torch.device) -> None:
1439:         """Shard per-channel C8 scales/offsets to this TP rank and pre-compute
1440:         BF16 BNSD antiquant tensors for FIA V1 decode fast path.
1441:         """
1442:         if hasattr(layer, "_c8_scales_prepared"):
1443:             return
1444: 
1445:         def _shard_and_reshape(raw: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
1446:             if raw.numel() == 1:
1447:                 return raw.to(device=device)
1448:             expected = self.num_kv_heads * self.head_size
1449:             if raw.numel() != expected:
1450:                 total_kv_heads = raw.numel() // self.head_size
1451:                 tp_rank = get_tensor_model_parallel_rank()
1452:                 tp_size = get_tensor_model_parallel_world_size()
1453:                 kv_head_start = tp_rank * total_kv_heads // tp_size
1454:                 raw = raw.view(total_kv_heads, self.head_size)[
1455:                     kv_head_start : kv_head_start + self.num_kv_heads
1456:                 ].contiguous()
1457:             return raw.view(1, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size).to(device=device)
1458: 
1459:         layer._c8_k_scale = _shard_and_reshape(layer.k_cache_scale.data)
1460:         layer._c8_k_offset = _shard_and_reshape(layer.k_cache_offset.data)
1461:         layer._c8_v_scale = _shard_and_reshape(layer.v_cache_scale.data)
1462:         layer._c8_v_offset = _shard_and_reshape(layer.v_cache_offset.data)
1463: 
1464:         bnsd = (1, self.num_kv_heads, 1, self.head_size)
1465:         layer._c8_k_aq_scale = layer._c8_k_scale.view(bnsd).contiguous()
1466:         layer._c8_k_aq_offset = layer._c8_k_offset.view(bnsd).contiguous()
1467:         layer._c8_v_aq_scale = layer._c8_v_scale.view(bnsd).contiguous()
1468:         layer._c8_v_aq_offset = layer._c8_v_offset.view(bnsd).contiguous()
1469: 
1470:         layer._c8_k_inv_scale = 1.0 / layer._c8_k_scale
1471:         layer._c8_v_inv_scale = 1.0 / layer._c8_v_scale
1472: 
1473:         layer._c8_scales_prepared = True
1474: 
1475:     def _dequant_paged_kv_to_dense(
1476:         self,
1477:         key: torch.Tensor,
1478:         value: torch.Tensor,
1479:         block_table: torch.Tensor,
1480:         seq_lens: list,
1481:         target_dtype: torch.dtype,
1482:         layer,
1483:     ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
1484:         """Gather paged INT8 KV blocks and dequantize."""
1485:         batch_size = block_table.shape[0]
1486:         block_size = key.shape[1]
1487:         H = key.shape[2]
1488:         max_blocks_per_seq = block_table.shape[1]
1489:         max_tokens_padded = max_blocks_per_seq * block_size
1490: 
1491:         flat_ids = block_table.reshape(-1)
1492:         gathered_k = key[flat_ids].view(batch_size, max_tokens_padded, H)
1493:         gathered_v = value[flat_ids].view(batch_size, max_tokens_padded, H)
1494: 
1495:         seq_lens_t = torch.tensor(seq_lens, dtype=torch.long, device=key.device)
1496:         positions = torch.arange(max_tokens_padded, dtype=torch.long, device=key.device)
1497:         valid_mask = (positions.unsqueeze(0) < seq_lens_t.unsqueeze(1)).view(-1)
1498: 
1499:         dense_k = gathered_k.view(-1, H)[valid_mask]
1500:         dense_v = gathered_v.view(-1, H)[valid_mask]
1501: 
1502:         dense_k = dense_k.view(-1, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
1503:         dense_v = dense_v.view(-1, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_size)
1504:         dense_k = (dense_k.to(target_dtype) - layer._c8_k_offset) * layer._c8_k_scale
1505:         dense_v = (dense_v.to(target_dtype) - layer._c8_v_offset) * layer._c8_v_scale
1506:         return dense_k, dense_v
1507: 
1508:     def _quantize_kv_to_int8(
1509:         self,
1510:         key: torch.Tensor,
1511:         value: torch.Tensor,
1512:         layer: AttentionLayer,
1513:         num_actual_tokens: int,
1514:     ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
1515:         """Quantize K/V from float to INT8 using static per-channel C8 scales."""
1516:         actual_key = key[:num_actual_tokens]
1517:         actual_value = value[:num_actual_tokens]
1518: 
1519:         k_int8 = torch.clamp(
1520:             torch.round(actual_key * layer._c8_k_inv_scale + layer._c8_k_offset),
1521:             -128,
1522:             127,
1523:         ).to(torch.int8)
1524:         v_int8 = torch.clamp(
1525:             torch.round(actual_value * layer._c8_v_inv_scale + layer._c8_v_offset),
1526:             -128,
1527:             127,
1528:         ).to(torch.int8)
1529:         return k_int8, v_int8
1530: 
1531:     def _forward_c8_decode(
1532:         self,
1533:         query: torch.Tensor,
1534:         attn_metadata: AscendMetadata,
1535:         output: torch.Tensor,
1536:         layer: AttentionLayer,
1537:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
1538:         """C8 decode via FIA V1 BNSD with native paged INT8 KV + perchannel antiquant."""
1539:         num_block, block_size, _, _ = self.key_cache.shape  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1540:         assert block_size % 32 == 0, f"C8 INT8 KV cache requires block_size to be a multiple of 32, got {block_size}"
1541:         key = self.key_cache.view(num_block, block_size, -1)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1542:         value = self.value_cache.view(num_block, block_size, -1)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1543:         batch_size = len(attn_metadata.seq_lens_list)
1544: 
1545:         attn_output, _ = torch_npu.npu_fused_infer_attention_score(
1546:             query[:batch_size].unsqueeze(2),
1547:             key,
1548:             value,
1549:             key_antiquant_scale=layer._c8_k_aq_scale,
1550:             key_antiquant_offset=layer._c8_k_aq_offset,
1551:             value_antiquant_scale=layer._c8_v_aq_scale,
1552:             value_antiquant_offset=layer._c8_v_aq_offset,
1553:             block_table=attn_metadata.block_tables,
1554:             actual_seq_lengths_kv=attn_metadata.seq_lens_list,
1555:             num_heads=self.num_heads,
1556:             num_key_value_heads=self.num_kv_heads,
1557:             input_layout="BNSD",
1558:             scale=self.scale,
1559:             block_size=block_size,
1560:             key_antiquant_mode=0,
1561:             value_antiquant_mode=0,
1562:             sparse_mode=0,

(Showing lines 1343-1562 of 1725. Use offset=1563 to continue.)

# vLLM Ascend Development Guidelines

This document provides instructions for contributors to the vLLM Ascend project. Please read and follow these guidelines to ensure code quality, maintainability, and consistency.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Setup and Environment](#setup-and-environment)
    - [Environment Variables](#environment-variables)
    - [Environment Variable Review Requirement](#environment-variable-review-requirement)
- [Testing](#testing)
    - [Unit and System Tests](#unit-and-system-tests)
    - [Running Tests](#running-tests)
- [Code Style](#code-style)
    - [Python Conventions](#python-conventions)
    - [Naming Conventions](#naming-conventions)
- [NPU-Specific Considerations](#npu-specific-considerations)
    - [Tensor item() Operations](#tensor-item-operations)
    - [Memory and Performance](#memory-and-performance)
- [Model and Plugin Architecture](#model-and-plugin-architecture)
    - [vLLM Ascend Plugin Architecture](#vllm-ascend-plugin-architecture)
    - [Patching Requirement](#patching-requirement)
    - [Model Runner Changes](#model-runner-changes)
- [Commit Messages and Pull Requests](#commit-messages-and-pull-requests)
    - [Commit Message Format](#commit-message-format)
- [Review Checklist](#review-checklist)
    - [Code Quality](#code-quality)
    - [Testing](#testing-1)
    - [Documentation](#documentation)
    - [NPU Considerations](#npu-considerations)
    - [Commit and PR](#commit-and-pr)
- [Quick Start for Contributors](#quick-start-for-contributors)
- [References](#references)

---

## Setup and Environment

### Environment Variables

All environment variables must be defined in `vllm_ascend/envs.py` using the centralized `env_variables` dictionary.

**Requirements:**

- Add documentation for each environment variable in the `env_variables` dict comment
- Specify default values and valid ranges
- Indicate whether the variable is sensitive (credentials, keys)

**Example:**

```python
import os

env_variables = {
    "VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_NZ": lambda: int(os.getenv("VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_NZ", 1)),
    # ...
}
```

**Never**: Hardcode environment variable names throughout the codebase. Reference them from the central module using `from vllm_ascend import envs`.

### Environment Variable Review Requirement

**Strict Review Required**: All new environment variables must undergo code review.

Reviewers must verify:

- The variable name follows the `VLLM_ASCEND_*` naming convention
- Default value is appropriate for all supported hardware
- Documentation is added to the `env_variables` dict
- The variable is used in a performance-critical path

---

## Testing

### Unit and System Tests

**Requirement**: All new functionality requires corresponding tests.

- **Unit Tests (UT)**: Located in `tests/ut/`, cover core logic, edge cases, and error conditions
- **System Tests (ST)**: Located in `tests/e2e/`, verify end-to-end behavior and integration points
- **Nightly Tests**: Include benchmarks for NPU-specific code paths in `tests/e2e/nightly/`

**Test Coverage Guidelines:**

- New features: Tests must cover happy path and failure modes
- Bug fixes: Tests must include a regression test for the bug
- Performance-critical code: Include benchmarks and performance regression tests

### Running Tests

```bash


# Run specific unit test file
pytest -sv tests/ut/ops/test_prepare_finalize.py

# Run specific unit test
pytest -sv tests/ut/ops/test_prepare_finalize.py::test_prepare_inputs

# Run NPU-specific tests (requires NPU hardware)
pytest -sv tests/e2e/singlecard/test_piecewise_res_consistency.py
```

**Requirement**: Run all tests locally before requesting review. Verify tests pass on NPU hardware for NPU-specific changes.

---

## Code Style

### Python Conventions

- **Imports**: All imports at the top of the file. Valid exceptions:
    - Circular imports (use inline imports)
    - Lazy loading for worker/isolation processes
    - Type-checking imports wrapped in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`

- **Global Variables**: Avoid new global variables. Pass dependencies explicitly through function parameters.

    **Allowed:**
    - Constants named `ALL_UPPER_CASE` (e.g., `MAX_BATCH_SIZE` in `envs.py`)
    - Immutable configuration objects

    **Requires Approval:**
    - Any new mutable global state

- **No Magic Numbers**: Use named constants with descriptive names:

    ```python
    # Bad
    if seq_len > 2048: ...

    # Good
    MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH = 2048
    if seq_len > MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH: ...
    ```

- **Descriptive Naming**: Use names that describe functionality, not implementation details.

    ```python
    # Bad
    is_deepseek_v3_r1
    flag1
    tmp_var

    # Good
    supports_dynamic_temperature
    uses_speculative_decoding
    ```

### Naming Conventions

- **Classes**: `PascalCase` (e.g., `NPUModelRunner`, `AscendSampler`, `ACLGraphManager`)
- **Functions/Methods**: `snake_case` (e.g., `forward_pass`, `compute_attention`)
- **Constants**: `ALL_UPPER_CASE` (e.g., `MAX_BATCH_SIZE`, `VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_NZ`)
- **Variables**: `snake_case` (e.g., `token_ids`, `sequence_lengths`)

---

## NPU-Specific Considerations

### Tensor item() Operations

**Warning**: `tensor.item()` operations cause synchronization overhead on NPU when the `tensor` is on device.

If the `tensor` is a device tensor, calling `item()` will triggers a synchronous data transfer from NPU to CPU. This can severely degrade performance in hot paths, causing `AsyncScheduler` to block here.

**Review Requirements:**

1. Profile performance impact before merging
2. Consider alternative patterns:
    - Keep values on device when possible
    - Batch operations to reduce sync frequency
    - Use device-side operations (e.g., `torch.argmax`, `torch.sum`)
3. Document when `item()` is unavoidable (e.g., logging, conditional logic)

**Example Patterns:**

```python
# Bad: In hot loop - causes sync per iteration
for tensor in tensors:
    value = tensor.item()

# Better: Batch operations - single sync
values = [t.item() for t in tensors]  # Single batch sync

# Good: Keep on device when possible
max_value = torch.max(tensor)  # No sync needed
if max_value > threshold:  # Comparison can stay on device
    ...
```

### Memory and Performance

Additional NPU-specific best practices:

- Avoid CPU-NPU memory transfers in hot paths
- Prefer in-place operations where safe (e.g., `x.add_()`, `x.mul_()`)
- Monitor memory fragmentation, especially for long-running processes
- Test with realistic workloads on actual NPU hardware (Ascend 910B/C)

---

## Model and Plugin Architecture

### vLLM Ascend Plugin Architecture

vLLM Ascend is a **hardware plugin** that integrates with upstream vLLM via the pluggable hardware interface. It does not add new model files directly.

**Required Pattern**: Model-specific functionality should be implemented via:

1. **Patching** (in `vllm_ascend/patch/`):
    - `vllm_ascend/patch/platform/` - Platform-level patches (distributed, scheduling)
    - `vllm_ascend/patch/worker/` - Worker-level patches (model-specific behavior)
    - Example: `patch_deepseek.py` modifies upstream Deepseek model behavior
    - Patch is not the best solution for all cases. Use it when necessary.

2. **Inheritance**:
    - `NPUModelRunner(GPUModelRunner)` - Extend vLLM model runner with NPU-specific behavior
    - `AscendSampler` - Extend vLLM sampler with NPU-specific operations
    - Add NPU-specific components via composition (e.g., `AclGraphManager`)
    - Custom Operators - NPU-specific custom operators (e.g., `AscendRMSNorm`)

3. **External upstream contributions** where appropriate

### Patching Requirement

**Strict Review Required**: All new patches must undergo thorough architectural review.

Reviewers must verify:

- The patch targets the correct upstream component
- The patch is minimal and focused
- Performance implications are understood
- A long-term plan exists for upstream contribution

**Example Patch Pattern:**

```python
# vllm_ascend/patch/worker/patch_deepseek.py
from vllm.model_executor.models.deepseek_v2 import DeepseekV2Model

def forward(self, input_ids, positions, ...):
    # NPU-specific forward implementation
    ...

DeepseekV2Model.forward = forward  # Patch upstream class
```

### Model Runner Changes

**Strict Review Required**: All new behaviors added to `model_runner` must undergo thorough architectural review.

Reviewers must verify:

- The necessity of the new behavior (why can't this be in a patch?)
- Performance implications on NPU hardware
- Compatibility with existing model implementations
- Long-term maintainability and test coverage

**NPU Model Runner Files:**

- `vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py` - vLLM v1 model runner
- `vllm_ascend/worker/v2/model_runner.py` - vLLM v2 model runner
- `vllm_ascend/_310p/model_runner_310p.py` - Ascend 310P model runner

---

## Commit Messages and Pull Requests

### Commit Message Format

Follow the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format and **must include a sign-off**:

```bash
git commit -s -m "<type>: <summary>" -m "<body - explaining what changed and why>"
```

Or using the full message format:

```txt
<type>: <summary>

<body - explaining what changed and why>

Signed-off-by: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
```

**Valid Types**: `feat`, `fix`, `perf`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `chore`

**Good Examples:**

```txt
feat(npu): add flash attention support for Ascend CANN

- Implements FlashAttention-2 kernel for NPU backend
- Reduces memory usage by 30% compared to baseline

fix(model_runner): correct padding token handling

- Fixes token padding that caused incorrect attention masks
- Addresses issue #1234

perf: avoid CPU-NPU sync in attention computation

- Inline computation to avoid tensor.item() calls
- Improves throughput by 15%
```

**Bad Examples:**

```txt
fix bug
add feature
update code
```

### Pull Request Title Format

PR titles should follow the format: `[Type][Module] Description`

- **Type**: The type of change (e.g., `CI`, `Doc`, `BugFix`, `Feat`, `Platform`, `Refactor`)
- **Module**: The affected module (optional, e.g., `Misc`, `Model`, `Worker`)
- **Description**: Brief description of the change

**Examples:**

- `[Doc][Misc] Update contribution guidelines`
- `[BugFix] Fix CPU binding logic`
- `[CI] Update image build workflow`

### Pull Request Template

When creating a PR, please follow the template in `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` and ensure the following sections are completed:

> **Note**: The PR description will be automatically updated by GitHub Actions to include vLLM version info at the bottom. If you update the PR description via API or CLI, make sure to preserve the `- vLLM version:` and `- vLLM main:` lines.

- **What this PR does / why we need it?** - Clearly describe the changes and their purpose
- **Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?** - Indicate if there are any user-visible changes
- **How was this patch tested?** - Describe how you tested the changes. Examples:
    - Unit tests added/updated: list the test files
    - Manual testing: provide the test steps and commands
    - CI testing: indicate if only CI verification is needed

---

## Review Checklist

Before merging, verify:

### Code Quality

- [ ] Code follows style guidelines (naming, imports, no magic numbers)
- [ ] No global state added without justification
- [ ] Patching pattern used correctly (if applicable)
- [ ] No direct model file additions

### Testing

- [ ] New tests added for new functionality (`tests/ut/` or `tests/e2e/`)
- [ ] Existing tests pass
- [ ] NPU-specific tests verified on actual hardware
- [ ] Performance benchmarks included where applicable

### Documentation

- [ ] Environment variables documented
- [ ] Public APIs documented
- [ ] User-facing changes reflected in docs

### NPU Considerations

- [ ] `tensor.item()` usage reviewed for performance impact
- [ ] No unnecessary CPU-NPU transfers in hot paths
- [ ] Memory usage verified on NPU hardware

### Commit and PR

- [ ] Commit messages are clear and descriptive, following Conventional Commits format
- [ ] **All commits are signed off** (`git commit -s`)
- [ ] PR is created from your fork repository, not directly from the main repository
- [ ] PR description is complete, following the PR template
- [ ] All review comments addressed

---

## Quick Start for Contributors

1. Install development dependencies: `pip install -e .[dev]`
2. Run tests: `pytest tests/`
3. Check linting: `ruff check vllm_ascend/`
4. Format code: `ruff format vllm_ascend/`
5. Make your changes following guidelines in this document
6. Add tests for new behavior
7. Run full test suite before committing
8. Commit with sign-off: `git commit -s`
9. Run linting check before pushing:
   ```bash
   bash format.sh ci
   ```
   > **Note**: This check is required for **all file types**, including markdown files. If `markdownlint` modifies files, re-add them with `git add` and commit again.
10. Push to your fork repository (NOT the main repository):

   ```bash
   git remote add myfork https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/vllm-ascend.git
   git push -u myfork your-branch-name
   ```

11. Create a PR from your fork to the main repository with clear description

---

## References

- [vLLM Hardware Plugin RFC](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/11162)
- [Documentation](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/ascend/en/latest/)
- [Contributors Guide](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/ascend/en/latest/community/contributors.html)

</system-reminder>

1585:         if num_decode_tokens > 0:
1586:             num_block, block_size, _, _ = self.key_cache.shape  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1587:             assert block_size % 32 == 0, (
1588:                 f"C8 INT8 KV cache requires block_size to be a multiple of 32, got {block_size}"
1589:             )
1590:             kv_k = self.key_cache.view(num_block, block_size, -1)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1591:             kv_v = self.value_cache.view(num_block, block_size, -1)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1592: 
1593:             attn_out, _ = torch_npu.npu_fused_infer_attention_score(
1594:                 query[:num_decode_tokens].unsqueeze(2),
1595:                 kv_k,
1596:                 kv_v,
1597:                 key_antiquant_scale=layer._c8_k_aq_scale,
1598:                 key_antiquant_offset=layer._c8_k_aq_offset,
1599:                 value_antiquant_scale=layer._c8_v_aq_scale,
1600:                 value_antiquant_offset=layer._c8_v_aq_offset,
1601:                 block_table=attn_metadata.block_tables[:num_decodes],
1602:                 actual_seq_lengths_kv=attn_metadata.seq_lens_list[:num_decodes],
1603:                 num_heads=self.num_heads,
1604:                 num_key_value_heads=self.num_kv_heads,
1605:                 input_layout="BNSD",
1606:                 scale=self.scale,
1607:                 block_size=block_size,
1608:                 key_antiquant_mode=0,
1609:                 value_antiquant_mode=0,
1610:                 sparse_mode=0,
1611:             )
1612:             output[:num_decode_tokens] = attn_out.squeeze(2)
1613: 
1614:         if attn_metadata.num_prefills > 0:
1615:             prefill_q = query[num_decode_tokens:num_tokens]
1616: 
1617:             prefill_seq_qlen = [
1618:                 actual_seq_qlen[i] - num_decode_tokens for i in range(num_decodes, len(actual_seq_qlen))
1619:             ]
1620: 
1621:             all_new_prefill = True
1622:             for i in range(num_decodes, len(attn_metadata.seq_lens_list)):
1623:                 q_start = actual_seq_qlen[i - 1] if i > 0 else 0
1624:                 qlen_i = actual_seq_qlen[i] - q_start
1625:                 if attn_metadata.seq_lens_list[i] > qlen_i:
1626:                     all_new_prefill = False
1627:                     break
1628: 
1629:             if all_new_prefill and float_key is not None and float_value is not None:
1630:                 prefill_k = float_key[num_decode_tokens:num_tokens]
1631:                 prefill_v = float_value[num_decode_tokens:num_tokens]
1632:                 prefill_seq_kvlen = prefill_seq_qlen
1633:             else:
1634:                 num_block, blk_size, _, _ = self.key_cache.shape  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1635:                 paged_k = self.key_cache.view(num_block, blk_size, -1)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1636:                 paged_v = self.value_cache.view(num_block, blk_size, -1)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1637:                 prefill_bt = attn_metadata.block_tables[num_decodes:]
1638:                 prefill_sl = attn_metadata.seq_lens_list[num_decodes:]
1639:                 prefill_k, prefill_v = self._dequant_paged_kv_to_dense(
1640:                     paged_k, paged_v, prefill_bt, prefill_sl, query.dtype, layer
1641:                 )
1642:                 prefill_seq_kvlen = torch.tensor(prefill_sl, dtype=torch.int32).cumsum(dim=0)
1643: 
1644:             # block_table is None for prefill; FIA ignores block_size in this case.
1645:             # Use cache block_size for consistency rather than a magic number.
1646:             cache_block_size = self.key_cache.shape[1]  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1647:             attn_out, _ = torch_npu.npu_fused_infer_attention_score(
1648:                 query=prefill_q,
1649:                 key=prefill_k,
1650:                 value=prefill_v,
1651:                 atten_mask=attn_metadata.attn_mask,
1652:                 block_table=None,
1653:                 input_layout="TND",
1654:                 block_size=cache_block_size,
1655:                 actual_seq_lengths=prefill_seq_qlen,
1656:                 actual_seq_lengths_kv=prefill_seq_kvlen,
1657:                 num_key_value_heads=self.num_kv_heads,
1658:                 num_heads=self.num_heads,
1659:                 scale=self.scale,
1660:                 sparse_mode=3,
1661:             )
1662:             n_prefill = num_tokens - num_decode_tokens
1663:             attn_out = attn_out.view(n_prefill, self.num_heads, self.head_size)
1664:             output[num_decode_tokens:num_tokens] = attn_out[:n_prefill]
1665: 
1666:         return output
1667: 
1668:     def _forward_c8_fused_infer_attention(
1669:         self,
1670:         query: torch.Tensor,
1671:         key: torch.Tensor,
1672:         value: torch.Tensor,
1673:         attn_metadata: AscendMetadata,
1674:         output: torch.Tensor,
1675:         layer: AttentionLayer,
1676:     ):
1677:         """C8 FIA V1 TND for prefill states (PrefillNoCache uses float KV directly,
1678:         PrefillCacheHit gathers + dequants paged INT8 KV).
1679:         """
1680:         key, value, block_size, block_table, actual_seq_lengths_kv = self._get_fia_params(key, value, attn_metadata)
1681:

1686:         if (

1698:                 key, value = self._dequant_paged_kv_to_dense(key, value, block_table, seq_lens, query.dtype, layer)
1699:                 block_table = None
1700:                 # block_table is None after dequant; FIA ignores block_size.
1701:                 # Use cache block_size for consistency rather than a magic number.
1702:                 block_size = self.key_cache.shape[1]  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

<path>D:\Desktop\Tuborquant\vllm-ascend-0.20.2rc1\vllm_ascend\quantization\methods\kv_c8.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import torch
2: from vllm.config import get_current_vllm_config
3: from vllm.distributed import get_tensor_model_parallel_rank, get_tensor_model_parallel_world_size
4: from vllm.logger import logger
5: 
6: from vllm_ascend.utils import AscendDeviceType, get_ascend_device_type
7: 
8: from .base import AscendAttentionScheme
9: from .registry import register_scheme
10: 
11: 
12: def _fa_quant_weight_loader(param: torch.Tensor, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor):
13:     """Weight loader for MLA-based C8 (FAKQuant) models."""
14:     if param.numel() == 1 and loaded_weight.numel() == 1:
15:         param.data.fill_(loaded_weight.item())
16:     else:
17:         tp_rank = get_tensor_model_parallel_rank()
18:         tp_size = get_tensor_model_parallel_world_size()
19:         shard_size = loaded_weight.shape[0] // tp_size
20:         loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(0, shard_size * tp_rank, shard_size)
21:         assert param.size() == loaded_weight.size(), (
22:             "[vllm-ascend/FAKQuant] Attempted to load weight "
23:             f"({loaded_weight.size()}) into parameter ({param.size()}) "
24:             f"when TP size is {tp_size} and TP rank is {tp_rank}."
25:         )
26: 
27:         param.data.copy_(loaded_weight)
28: 
29: 
30: @register_scheme("FAKQuant", "attention")
31: class AscendFAQuantAttentionMethod:
32:     def __init__(self):
33:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
34:         config = vllm_config.model_config.hf_config
35:         self.kv_lora_rank = getattr(config, "kv_lora_rank", 0)
36:         self.qk_rope_head_dim = getattr(config, "qk_rope_head_dim", 0)
37: 
38:     def create_weights(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
39:         extra_module_names = ["fa_q", "fa_k", "fa_v"]
40:         for name in extra_module_names:
41:             setattr(layer, name, torch.nn.Module())
42:         params_dict = {}
43:         dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()
44:         params_dict["fa_q.scale"] = torch.empty((layer.num_heads, 1), dtype=dtype)
45:         params_dict["fa_k.scale"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=dtype)
46:         params_dict["fa_v.scale"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=dtype)
47:         params_dict["fa_q.offset"] = torch.empty((layer.num_heads, 1), dtype=torch.int8)
48:         params_dict["fa_k.offset"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=torch.int8)
49:         params_dict["fa_v.offset"] = torch.empty((layer.num_kv_heads, 1), dtype=torch.int8)
50: 
51:         for name, weight in params_dict.items():
52:             module_name, weight_name = name.rsplit(".", 1)
53:             module = getattr(layer, module_name)
54:             weight_param = torch.nn.Parameter(weight, requires_grad=False)
55:             module.register_parameter(weight_name, weight_param)
56:             # When loading weights, segment them according to TP
57:             weight_param.weight_loader = _fa_quant_weight_loader
58: 
59:     def process_weights_after_loading(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
60:         fa_k_scale = torch.squeeze(layer.fa_k.scale).unsqueeze(0)
61:         layer.fak_descale_float = torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale.to(torch.float), requires_grad=False)
62:         layer.fak_descale = torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale, requires_grad=False)
63:         if get_ascend_device_type() == AscendDeviceType.A5:
64:             layer.fak_descale_reciprocal = 1.0 / torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale.to(torch.float), requires_grad=False)
65:         else:
66:             layer.fak_descale_reciprocal = 1.0 / torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_scale, requires_grad=False)
67:         fa_k_offset = torch.squeeze(layer.fa_k.offset).unsqueeze(0)
68:         layer.fak_offset = torch.nn.Parameter(fa_k_offset.to(layer.fak_descale.dtype), requires_grad=False)
69: 
70:         repeated_quant_kscale = fa_k_scale.repeat(self.kv_lora_rank)
71:         layer.quant_kscale = repeated_quant_kscale.view(1, self.kv_lora_rank)
72:         layer.quant_kscale = 1.0 / torch.nn.Parameter(layer.quant_kscale.to(torch.float), requires_grad=False)
73: 
74: 
75: @register_scheme("INT8_DYNAMIC", "attention")
76: class AscendSFAQuantAttentionMethod:
77:     def __init__(self):
78:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
79:         config = vllm_config.model_config.hf_config
80:         self.index_head_dim = config.index_head_dim
81: 
82:     def create_weights(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
83:         extra_module_names = ["indexer"]
84:         for name in extra_module_names:
85:             setattr(layer, name, torch.nn.Module())
86:         params_dict = {}
87:         params_dict["indexer.q_rot"] = torch.empty((self.index_head_dim, self.index_head_dim), dtype=torch.float32)
88:         params_dict["indexer.k_rot"] = torch.empty((self.index_head_dim, self.index_head_dim), dtype=torch.float32)
89:         for name, weight in params_dict.items():
90:             module_name, weight_name = name.split(".")
91:             module = getattr(layer, module_name)
92:             weight_param = torch.nn.Parameter(weight, requires_grad=False)
93:             module.register_parameter(weight_name, weight_param)
94: 
95:     def process_weights_after_loading(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
96:         pass
97: 
98: 
99: def _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader(param: torch.nn.Parameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor) -> None:
100:     """Weight loader for dense-attention C8 KV cache scales/offsets."""
101:     loaded_weight = loaded_weight.squeeze()
102:     if param.data.shape != loaded_weight.shape:
103:         param.data = loaded_weight.to(param.dtype).clone()
104:     else:
105:         param.data.copy_(loaded_weight)
106: 
107: 
108: class AscendC8KVCacheAttentionMethod(AscendAttentionScheme):
109:     """C8 INT8 KV cache quantization for dense-attention models (e.g. Qwen3)."""
110: 
111:     def __init__(self, quant_description: dict, prefix: str):
112:         self.quant_description = quant_description
113:         self.prefix = prefix
114:         vllm_config = get_current_vllm_config()
115:         self.is_kv_producer = False
116:         if vllm_config.kv_transfer_config is not None:
117:             self.is_kv_producer = vllm_config.kv_transfer_config.is_kv_producer
118: 
119:     def create_weights(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
120:         # Returns int8 if the P node is not a PD detachment node.
121:         if not self.is_kv_producer:
122:             logger.info_once(
123:                 "[vllm-ascend/C8_KV] KV cache producer is disabled; setting kv_cache_torch_dtype to torch.int8."
124:             )
125:             layer.kv_cache_torch_dtype = torch.int8
126:         # Upgrade impl to the C8-specific subclass so the C8 forward path is always used.
127:         if hasattr(layer, "impl"):
128:             from vllm_ascend.attention.attention_v1 import AscendC8AttentionBackendImpl
129: 
130:             layer.impl.__class__ = AscendC8AttentionBackendImpl
131:         dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()
132:         layer.k_cache_scale = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.ones(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
133:         layer.k_cache_scale.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
134:         layer.k_cache_offset = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
135:         layer.k_cache_offset.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
136:         layer.v_cache_scale = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.ones(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
137:         layer.v_cache_scale.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
138:         layer.v_cache_offset = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(1, dtype=dtype), requires_grad=False)
139:         layer.v_cache_offset.weight_loader = _c8_kv_scale_weight_loader
140: 
141:     def process_weights_after_loading(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
142:         layer.k_cache_scale.data = layer.k_cache_scale.data.flatten()
143:         layer.k_cache_offset.data = layer.k_cache_offset.data.flatten()
144:         layer.v_cache_scale.data = layer.v_cache_scale.data.flatten()
145:         layer.v_cache_offset.data = layer.v_cache_offset.data.flatten()
146: 
147:     def apply(
148:         self,
149:         layer: torch.nn.Module,
150:         query: torch.Tensor,
151:         key: torch.Tensor,
152:         value: torch.Tensor,
153:         kv_cache,
154:         attn_metadata,
155:         attn_type,
156:         scale,
157:         output,
158:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
159:         err_msg = (
160:             "[vllm-ascend/C8_KV] AscendC8KVCacheAttentionMethod.apply should "
161:             "not be called. C8 KV cache quantization is handled by the "
162:             "attention backend."
163:         )
164:         raise RuntimeError(err_msg)

161: class AscendKVCacheMethod(BaseKVCacheMethod):
162:     """KVCache method for Ascend quantization.
163: 
164:     This wrapper class delegates to the actual attention quantization scheme.
165: 
166:     Args:
167:         scheme: The attention quantization scheme instance.
168:     """
169: 
170:     def __init__(self, scheme: AscendAttentionScheme) -> None:
171:         self.quant_method = scheme
172: 
173:     def create_weights(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
174:         # Different from linear method, there are no weight processing/slicing
175:         # steps for attention in vllm. So the whole process of create weights
176:         # is hidden into the specific quant method.
177:         self.quant_method.create_weights(layer)
178: 
179:     def process_weights_after_loading(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> None:
180:         self.quant_method.process_weights_after_loading(layer)
181:

183:         self,
184:         layer: torch.nn.Module,
185:         query: torch.Tensor,
186:         key: torch.Tensor,
187:         value: torch.Tensor,
188:         kv_cache,
189:         attn_metadata,
190:         attn_type,
191:         scale,
192:         output,
193:     ) -> torch.Tensor:
194:         return self.quant_method.apply(layer, query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, attn_type, scale, output)

3565:         for group_kv_cache_spec in kv_cache_config.kv_cache_groups:
3566:             group_spec = group_kv_cache_spec.kv_cache_spec
3567:             for layer_name in group_kv_cache_spec.layer_names:
3568:                 if isinstance(group_spec, UniformTypeKVCacheSpecs):
3569:                     layer_kv_cache_spec[layer_name] = group_spec.kv_cache_specs[layer_name]
3570:                 else:
3571:                     layer_kv_cache_spec[layer_name] = group_spec
3572:         return layer_kv_cache_spec
3573: 
3574:     def _get_attention_kv_cache_dims(self, layer_name: str, kv_cache_spec: AttentionSpec) -> tuple[int, int]:
3575:         if isinstance(kv_cache_spec, MLAAttentionSpec):
3576:             attn_layers = get_layers_from_vllm_config(
3577:                 self.vllm_config,
3578:                 AttentionLayerBase,
3579:                 [layer_name],
3580:             )
3581:             attn_layer = attn_layers[layer_name]
3582:             if isinstance(attn_layer, MLAAttention):
3583:                 # DeepSeek MLA: K=kv_lora_rank, V=qk_rope_head_dim
3584:                 return attn_layer.kv_lora_rank, attn_layer.qk_rope_head_dim
3585:             # CacheOnlyAttentionLayer uses MLAAttentionSpec but isn't MLAAttention
3586:             if isinstance(attn_layer, CacheOnlyAttentionLayer):
3587:                 return kv_cache_spec.head_size, kv_cache_spec.head_size
3588:             raise TypeError(
3589:                 f"Expected MLAAttention layer for {layer_name}, got {type(attn_layer).__name__}."
3590:             )
3591: 
3592:         head_size_v = kv_cache_spec.head_size_v if hasattr(kv_cache_spec, "head_size_v") else kv_cache_spec.head_size
3593:         return kv_cache_spec.head_size, head_size_v
3594:

3595:     def _allocate_kv_cache_tensors(self, kv_cache_config: KVCacheConfig) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
3596:         """
3597:         Initializes the KV cache buffer with the correct size. The buffer needs
3598:         to be reshaped to the desired shape before being used by the models.
3599: 
3600:         NOTE: To support prefill disaggregation, we need to split kvcache tensor into
3601:         k_cache and v cache, and the addr of both are aligned by 2M
3602: 
3603:         Args:
3604:             kv_cache_config: The KV cache config
3605:         Returns:
3606:             dict[str, torch.Tensor]: A map between layer names to their
3607:             corresponding memory buffer for KV cache.
3608:             dict[str, tuple(torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor)] A map between layer names
3609:             to their corresponding memory buffer for K cache and V cache.
3610:         """
3611:         # init kv cache tensors
3612:         kv_cache_raw_tensors: dict[str, torch.Tensor | torch.Tensor | None | None] = {}
3613:         # prefill disaggregation need the addr of cache tensor be aligned with 2M
3614:         alignment = 2 * 1024 * 1024
3615:         layer_kv_cache_spec = self._get_layer_kv_cache_specs(kv_cache_config)
3616:         # If some tensors are shared by linear layers and attention layers,
3617:         # the same tensor format must be maintained even if some layers
3618:         # have only linear or attention layers, for example, the mtp layer.
3619:         self.hybrid_with_attn_and_mamba = False
3620:         for kv_cache_tensor in kv_cache_config.kv_cache_tensors:
3621:             use_mamba, use_attn = False, False
3622:             for layer_name in kv_cache_tensor.shared_by:
3623:                 if isinstance(layer_kv_cache_spec[layer_name], MambaSpec):
3624:                     use_mamba = True
3625:                 if isinstance(layer_kv_cache_spec[layer_name], AttentionSpec):
3626:                     use_attn = True
3627:             self.hybrid_with_attn_and_mamba = self.hybrid_with_attn_and_mamba or (use_mamba and use_attn)
3628:             for idx in range(len(kv_cache_tensor.shared_by)):
3629:                 layer_name = kv_cache_tensor.shared_by[idx]
3630:                 # Single tensor path for: mamba, hybrid attn-mamba, or cache_only_layers
3631:                 if (
3632:                     "linear_attn" in layer_name
3633:                     or self.hybrid_with_attn_and_mamba
3634:                     or "cache_only_layers" in layer_name

3753:     def _adjust_kv_layout(
3754:         self,
3755:         raw_tensor: torch.Tensor,
3756:         kv_cache_shape_list: list[int],
3757:         kv_cache_dtype_list: list[int],
3758:         page_size_bytes: int,
3759:     ):
3760:         reshaped_kv_tensors = []
3761:         storage_offset_bytes = raw_tensor.storage_offset()
3762:         for shape, dtype in zip(kv_cache_shape_list, kv_cache_dtype_list):
3763:             dtype_size = get_dtype_size(dtype)
3764:             num_element_per_page = (
3765:                 page_size_bytes // dtype_size
3766:             )
3767: 
3768:             stride = torch.empty(shape).stride()
3769:             target_stride = (num_element_per_page, *stride[1:])
3770:             assert storage_offset_bytes % dtype_size == 0
3771:             tensor = torch.as_strided(
3772:                 raw_tensor.view(dtype),
3773:                 size=shape,
3774:                 stride=target_stride,
3775:                 storage_offset=storage_offset_bytes // dtype_size,
3776:             )
3777:             reshaped_kv_tensors.append(tensor)
3778:             storage_offset_bytes += stride[0] * dtype_size
3779:         return reshaped_kv_tensors
3780:

3918:                                 attn_tensor_page_size = int(np.prod(kv_cache_shape[1:])) * get_dtype_size(
3919:                                     current_kv_cache_spec.dtype
3920:                                 )

3923:                                 raw_k_tensor = raw_kv_tensor[:attn_tensor_page_size]
3924:                                 raw_v_tensor = raw_kv_tensor[attn_tensor_page_size:]
3925:                             else:

3928:                                     current_kv_cache_spec.dtype

3931:                                     current_kv_cache_spec.dtype

3934:                                 raw_kv_tensor = raw_k_tensor[conv_block_padding_size:]
3935:                                 raw_k_tensor = raw_kv_tensor[:nope_page_size]
3936:                                 raw_v_tensor = raw_kv_tensor[nope_page_size:]
3937:                     else:
3938:                         kv_cache_shape = attn_backend.get_kv_cache_shape(
3939:                             num_blocks,
3940:                             current_kv_cache_spec.block_size,
3941:                             current_kv_cache_spec.num_kv_heads,
3942:                             current_kv_cache_spec.head_size,

3948:                         else:
3949:                             v_shape = k_shape
3950:                     else:
3951:                         # k_cache: nope_cache    v_cache: rope_cache
3952:                         mla_num_blocks, mla_block_size, num_kv_heads, _ = kv_cache_shape
3953:                         k_dim, v_dim = self._get_attention_kv_cache_dims(layer_name, current_kv_cache_spec)
3954:                         k_shape = (
3955:                             mla_num_blocks,
3956:                             mla_block_size,
3957:                             num_kv_heads,
3958:                             k_dim,
3959:                         )
3960:                         v_shape = (
3961:                             mla_num_blocks,
3962:                             mla_block_size,
3963:                             num_kv_heads,
3964:                             v_dim,
3965:                         )
3966:                     k_cache_dtype = v_cache_dtype = current_kv_cache_spec.dtype
3967:                     if enable_fa_quant(self.vllm_config):
3968:                         k_cache_dtype, v_cache_dtype = self.vllm_config.quant_config.get_kv_quant_dtype(
3969:                             layer_name, current_kv_cache_spec.dtype, self.model_config
3970:                         )

3972:                     v_cache = raw_v_tensor.view(v_cache_dtype).view(v_shape)
3973: 
3974:                     if self.use_sparse:
3975:                         dsa_k_cache_shape = (
3976:                             num_blocks,
3977:                             current_kv_cache_spec.block_size,
3978:                             current_kv_cache_spec.num_kv_heads,
3979:                             self.model_config.hf_text_config.index_head_dim,
3980:                         )
3981:                         if current_sparse_c8:
3982:                             # dsa_k
3983:                             dsa_k_cache = raw_dsa_k_tensor.view(self.c8_k_cache_dtype).view(dsa_k_cache_shape)
3984:                             # dsa_k_scale
3985:                             dsa_k_scale_cache_shape = (
3986:                                 num_blocks,
3987:                                 current_kv_cache_spec.block_size,
3988:                                 current_kv_cache_spec.num_kv_heads,
3989:                                 1,
3990:                             )
3991:                             assert raw_dsa_k_scale_tensor is not None
3992:                             dsa_k_scale_cache = (
3993:                                 raw_dsa_k_scale_tensor
3994:                                 .view(self.c8_k_scale_cache_dtype)
3995:                                 .view(dsa_k_scale_cache_shape)
3996:                             )
3997:                             kv_caches[layer_name] = (k_cache, v_cache, dsa_k_cache, dsa_k_scale_cache)
3998:                         else:

4000:                             dsa_k_cache = raw_dsa_k_tensor.view(current_kv_cache_spec.dtype).view(dsa_k_cache_shape)

4002:                     else:

4004:                 elif isinstance(current_kv_cache_spec, MambaSpec):

4006:                     assert raw_tensor is not None

4008:                     num_blocks = raw_tensor.numel() // current_kv_cache_spec.page_size_bytes

4011:                     # `num_blocks` is the number of blocks the model runner can use.

4015:                     # different memory capacities, `num_blocks` can be different on
